title: M23武装在戈马以北发动大规模攻势
desc: M23反政府武装在北基伍省戈马以北地区发动协同攻势，使用重武器和无人机。FARDC在联刚稳定团支援下组织防御。联合国报告称新一轮冲突导致超过15万平民流离失所，人道主义形势严峻。

title: M23占领萨凯镇东部阵地，逼近戈马市
desc: M23在北基伍省萨凯镇附近突破FARDC防线，占领多个战略高地，距离戈马市仅25公里。Wazalendo民兵协助FARDC建立新防线。戈马市居民大规模恐慌撤离。

actor1: CODECO
title: CODECO武装在伊图里省袭击村庄致47死
desc: CODECO民兵在伊图里省朱古地区多个村庄发动协同袭击，主要针对赫马族平民。袭击者使用砍刀和轻武器，焚烧超过200间房屋。该事件是伊图里省2026年以来最严重的族群暴力事件。

actor1: ADF
title: ADF在北基伍省贝尼地区发动爆炸袭击
desc: 民主同盟军(ADF)在贝尼地区一个市场附近引爆两枚IED，并随后开枪射击逃离人群。袭击造成至少23名平民死亡，30余人受伤。ADF已宣誓效忠伊斯兰国(ISCAP)。

title: M23控制区据报发生针对平民暴力事件
desc: 联合国人权高专办收到报告称M23在马西西地区控制区对拒绝合作的平民实施暴力。具体细节正在核实中。M23否认相关指控。

actor1: FARDC
title: 刚果(金)政府军在南基伍省大规模增兵
desc: 刚果(金)武装部队向南基伍省部署额外三个营的兵力，以应对M23向南扩张的威胁。卫星图像显示在布卡武以北约60公里处建立新的前线作战基地。南基伍省与北基伍省交界处紧张局势加剧。

actor1: FARDC
title: FARDC与M23在鲁丘鲁地区激战
desc: 刚果(金)武装部队在联刚稳定团火力支援下对M23控制的鲁丘鲁地区发起反攻。双方在多个村庄展开拉锯战，FARDC声称收复两个村庄，但M23否认。

actor1: ADF
title: ADF在布滕博郊区发动伏击
desc: ADF武装分子在布滕博市郊区伏击了一辆民用巴士，造成11名乘客死亡。当地民间社会呼吁政府加强该地区的安全措施。

actor1: 政府
title: 金沙萨当局要求加速MONUSCO撤军进程
desc: 刚果(金)政府在金沙萨再次要求联刚稳定团(MONUSCO)加速撤离进程，声称国家主权不容长期依赖外部安全力量。但东部安全形势恶化使得撤军时间表一再推迟。联合国秘书长呼吁负责任过渡。

actor1: Zaire Militia
title: 扎伊尔民兵与CODECO在伊图里省交火
desc: 亲赫马族的扎伊尔民兵与伦杜族为主的CODECO武装在朱古地区发生冲突，双方使用迫击炮和重机枪。此次冲突是伊图里族群紧张的最新升级，约12,000名平民逃离冲突区域。

actor1: 矿工工会
title: 上加丹加省矿工就安全条件举行罢工
desc: 上加丹加省卢本巴希矿区的矿工工会举行罢工，抗议矿井安全条件恶劣和薪资拖欠。该省是刚果钴和铜的主要产区，罢工影响了部分矿场运营。

actor1: ADF
title: ADF在伊鲁穆地区绑架平民
desc: ADF武装在伊鲁穆地区袭击了一个村庄，绑架14名平民并焚烧多处房屋。当地村民报告称ADF近期活动频率显著增加。

actor1: Mai-Mai
title: Mai-Mai民兵在坦噶尼喀省抢劫村庄
desc: 多个Mai-Mai派系在坦噶尼喀省偏远村庄发动协同抢劫和袭击，目标为当地商人和农民。FARDC已派遣增援部队前往该地区。坦噶尼喀省长期以来是多个Mai-Mai团体的活动区域。

actor1: Mai-Mai
title: Mai-Mai民兵在南基伍省乌维拉地区与军方交火
desc: 地方Mai-Mai民兵在乌维拉地区袭击FARDC巡逻队，交火中双方各有伤亡。该地区多个Mai-Mai团体对政府驻军不满，认为其未能有效保护社区免受跨境袭击。

actor1: 民众
title: 金沙萨民众抗议东部安全形势恶化
desc: 金沙萨数百名市民在议会大楼外和平集会，抗议东部省份持续恶化的安全形势和政府应对不力。抗议和平进行，无暴力事件发生。

actor1: LRA残部
title: 上韦莱省LRA残部袭击致15平民死亡
desc: 上帝抵抗军(LRA)残余力量在上韦莱省偏远地区袭击村庄，绑架12人（包括5名儿童）。乌干达军方配合FARDC部队展开搜剿。LRA虽已式微，但残余分子仍在该省与中非共和国边境流窜。

actor1: CODECO
title: CODECO攻击伊图里省北部军事哨所
desc: CODECO派系在马哈吉地区对FARDC军事哨所发动攻击，双方交火约3小时。FARDC最终击退进攻，但付出了伤亡代价。

actor1: FDLR
title: FDLR残部在南基伍省菲齐地区抢劫村庄
desc: 卢旺达解放民主力量(FDLR)残余人员在菲齐地区偏远村庄实施抢劫，与当地民众发生冲突致8人死亡。FDLR近年实力虽大幅削弱，但仍在偏远林区活动。

actor1: FARDC
title: FARDC在卡米纳军事基地开展大规模集训
desc: FARDC在上洛马米省卡米纳军事基地开展大规模新兵集训，旨在补充东部前线损失。南非和布隆迪军事教官参与训练。该基地是刚果(金)最大的军事训练设施之一。

title: 【历史】2025年11月—M23夺取萨凯镇
desc: 2025年11月，M23武装在北基伍省发动大规模攻势，经过一周激战后夺取战略要地萨凯镇，威胁戈马市。这是自2012-2013年M23上次叛乱以来最严重的军事升级。联合国安理会召开紧急会议。

desc: On 17 May 2025, a riot broke out over a land conflict between two local families in Kibungie, coded to the chef-lieu Kabinda (Kabinda, Lomami). actor1: M23: March 23 Movement
title: Around 13 May 2025, M23-RDF arrested, raped and killed around 107 residents in Ngangi III (Bukumu, Nyiragongo, Nord-Kivu) and Ngangi II (coded separat
desc: Around 13 May 2025, M23-RDF arrested, raped and killed around 107 residents in Ngangi III (Bukumu, Nyiragongo, Nord-Kivu) and Ngangi II (coded separately). M23 searched houses and arrested people based off suspicions of affiliation with FARDC or Wazalendo, because they did not have IDs, or because of their hairstyle. All arrested were brought to Don Bosco stadium, where those able to prove their 'innocence' were released while others were brought to an unknown destination. 107 fatalities split as follows: Ngangi II (54); Ngangi III (53). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC36903)

desc: On 12 May 2025, artisanal miners beat up employees and vandalized goods and infrastructure at Shambara mine, exploited by COMAKAT cooperative, in Kawama village (Kipushi, Haut-Katanga). They damaged the dispensary, beat up medical staff and other agents, smashed the windscreens of trucks transporting mining products, and ransacked the offices of the COMAKAT mining cooperative. The mob was led by miners who were initiating a petition to claim their rights. Security services arrested 30 of the rioters. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC36886)

FARDC were escorting a convoy which was ambushed by the unidentified armed group. The unidentified armed group killed 5 FARDC. They also abducted a Chinese civilian working for a cement plant and looted weapons. 5 fatalities. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC35727)

desc: On 22 April 2025, overnight, FARDC attacked civilians in Kotakoli (Mobayi-Mbongo, Nord-Ubangi), after two soldiers were hit by lightning and died. FARDC accused residents of being responsible for lightning strikes and started looting shops and shooting at residents. A group of civilians seized weapons and shot back, killing one FARDC. Around 5 May, the Kotakoli Resident's Association demanded the relocation of soldiers there, following the previous events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC35673)

desc: Around 10 April 2025 (between 2 April - 20 April), M23 attacked Kibua (Walikale, Nord-Kivu), Kibati and Mikumbi (coded separately). In Kibua, the group killed at least 12 people, including two women. Most of them were beheaded or shot by guns. At least 83 fatalities, 82 CODECO and 1 civilian. 82 fatalities split across two days, coded as 41. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC35236)

desc: On 22 February 2025, suspected M23 killed at least 17 young men, and their bodies were found in Goma - Katindo (Goma, Nord-Kivu). Some sources (including the government), mention that the men were drinking and consuming drugs when M23 surprised them. Others mentioned a failed attempt of recruitment by M23. M23 spokesman stated that this crime 'was part of a sweeping and cleaning operation' and that these men were 'military family members, members of militia or drug users'. M23 were accused of committing sexual abuse against some young girls. One day later (23/02/2025), M23 was seen picking up some bodies and carrying them in their vehicles. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34970)

desc: On 18 February 2025, for the fourth of at least five consecutive days, FARDC and Wazalendo clashed against each other in Uvira (Uvira, Sud-Kivu). The reason of these clashes was that Wazalendo requested FARDC to leave their war material to them before leaving towards Kalemie. 17 fatalities including civilians, Wazalendo, and FARDC split across two days, coded as 9. FARDC allegedly also ransacked telephones and money, and damaged several houses. The population in Uvira left massively towards Burundi, Hauts Plateaux, Kalemie and Moba. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34862)

Wazalendo were trying to disarm or stop FARDC from retreating or deserting, by boat towards Kalemie. FARDC allegedly refused to hand out their weapons which caused the clash. 12 fatalities (fatalities coded according to Radio Okapi). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34826)

desc: On 15 February 2025, for the first of at least five consecutive days, Wazalendo clashed against FARDC in Uvira (Uvira, Sud-Kivu). 18 fatalities. The General Hospital of Uvira came under crossfire, forcing Medical activities to be halted, and patients were forced to take shelter to avoid being hit. Armed men also entered the hospital premises, moving around and shooting inside. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34827)

Killing and capturing an unspecified numbers of RDF soldiers. The FARDC seized RDF military equipment, such as ballistic Kevlar helmets, bullet-proof jackets, rocket-propelled grenades, mortar shell containers, anti-personnel grenades, and AK type assault rifles, and VHF radios. Unspecified fatalities coded as 10. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC38864)

desc: On 11 February 2025, overnight, CODECO attacked civilians in Lodja village and other Hema villages, near Djaiba IDP camp in Djaiba groupement (Walendu-Djatsi, Djugu, Ituri), killing more than 80 people (final death toll). They also set 50 houses on fire. This attack was in reprisal for an earlier attack on Lendu civilians on 9 February (coded separately). Residents of Lodja and neighboring villages fled. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34738)

desc: On 10 February 2025, overnight, CODECO-URDPC militants (in large numbers) attacked civilians in three locations: Laudjo, Lindu and Lodja, all in Djaiba groupement, coded to Djaiba (Djugu, Ituri). The victims were mainly IDPs of the Hema ethnic group staying with host families who had fled other CODECO attacks in the days prior. The attackers set people on fire, shot bullets and beheaded residents. At least 58 people were killed, including 28 children, with many burned alive in their homes. 9 people injured, and 49 houses were set on fire. FARDC said the attack was in reprisal for an attack committed by the Zaire militia days earlier. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34736)

desc: Around 6 February 2025 (as reported), residents of Bolembe clashed with Yalombuka residents in several villages of Yahuma territory, coded to territorial capital, Yahuma (Yahuma, Tshopo). 2 people were killed and several others injured. The clash involved machetes and stemmed from a conflict over customary succession and logging tension by Booming company, and unequal distribution of logging benefits, which had fueled rivalries between villages. These conflicts are further exploited by local political actors. 20 soldiers intervened to calm down the situation. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34650)

desc: On 1 January 2025, police killed 2 Chinese employees of the Crec 6 company and injured another in Mwene-Ditu (Mwene-Ditu, Lomami) during an altercation. The cause of the incident remains unclear. However, witnesses alleged the incident arose from a dispute over cooked beef for the end of year festivities, while others claimed the policeman, who had been with the Chinese in Mbuji-Mayi last week, stole a large sum of money after killing them. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34295)

This incident occurred after gunfire exchanges in Largu and Okapi the previous day (coded separately). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34249)

The two officers were burned after their killing. Other FARDC soldiers were injured and taken to the hospital. It is not clear if a clash occurred between the FARDC and the Mayi Mayi. At least 17 militia men were killed and 4 were captured. At least 13 weapons were recovered, and several ammunition. Population has moved fleeing violence in direction of Popokabaka and Kasongolunda. At least 20 fatalities split across two days, coded to 10. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34129)

(来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34111)

actor1: Rioters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 4 December 2024, for the fourth of four consecutive days, artisanal miners from various parts of Lualaba clashed with police at the Tondo mining co
desc: On 4 December 2024, for the fourth of four consecutive days, artisanal miners from various parts of Lualaba clashed with police at the Tondo mining concession, in the Tshipuki neighborhood of Kolwezi (Kolwezi, Haut-Katanga). 1 artisanal miner was killed after climbing onto a speeding truck that bypassed barricades on Joseph Kabila Road set up by rioters. 5 police officers were also injured by rioters who were forcing to access Tondo mining concession. Several company properties of the company were looted, including warehouses with an advanced laboratory, were looted, and over 100,000 drilling samples critical to the Nyoka and Tshipuki projects were destroyed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34065)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces

desc: On 3 December 2024, ADF killed at least 9 civilians, including 7 women and a child in Bakila-Tenambo neighborhood, Oicha (Beni, Nord-Kivu). 4-5 civilians were injured and unspecified number are still missing. At least 10-15 houses and shops were looted before being set on fire, along with motorbikes. Several residents fled to safe areas, including the commercial center of Oicha. The attackers were attempting to reclaim their former strongholds in the Graben region. FARDC intervened to repelled the attack (coded separately). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34067)

M23 responded, throwing two RLM bombs in Alimbongo. Unspecified number of fatalities coded as 10. Military equipment was also recovered. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34082)

desc: On 18 November 2024, two groups of demonstrators held protests in Boende (Boende, Tshuapa). The first group of protesters called for the removal from office of the President and Rapporteur of the National Assembly while the second group opposed the first. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC34013)

desc: On 15 November 2024, Wazalendo clashed and captured Butale (also spelled Butare) (Bashali, Masisi, Nord-Kivu), following clashes with M23. Around 26 M23 were killed. M23 attacked a position held by Wazalendo, prompting retaliation. The M23 elements retreated, with Wazalendo successfully taking control of Butale after setting M23 camp on fire. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC33958)

actor1: Kalenda Kanyi Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Lubenga Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)

desc: On 14 November 2024, Kalenda Kanyi communal militia clashed with Lubenga communal militia in the Bekalebwe sector, Lomani province (event coded to Lomani capital, Kabinda, Kabinda, Lomami). 2 people were killed and several others injured. Over 200 houses were destroyed in the villages of Lubenga and Laenda-Kanyi. All residents sought refuge in the bush. The conflict arose from a long-standing land dispute over a savannah between the two communities. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC33960)

actor1: Rioters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 13 November 2024, artisanal miners clashed with police in the mining concessions of Kambove (Haut-Katanga). At least 4 artisanal miners were killed
desc: On 13 November 2024, artisanal miners clashed with police in the mining concessions of Kambove (Haut-Katanga). At least 4 artisanal miners were killed. The incidents began when an artisanal miner illegally entered the Kambove Mining concession, owned by a Chinese company, demanding a workday, and was killed by police. His colleagues then attacked the local police headquarters, setting buildings, vehicles, and a hearse on fire, while ransacking offices and the peace court. Several buildings, including the administrator's residence and the head of transportation's vehicle, were also damaged. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC33986)

desc: On 13 November 2024, residents of Bondo-centre lynched and killed 2 civilians, a man and a woman, in Bondo (Wamba, Bas-Uele). The victims were burned alive two days after being accused of murdering two businessmen in the mining sector of Yakpa. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC33985)

RED-Tabara said 45 FDNB and Mayi Mayi were killed, including the deputy commander of the FDNB contingent in Sud-Kivu. RED-Tabara also said 32 were 'severely injured' and evacuated to a place called Guantanamo to Bujumbura. The Burundian government denied. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC33899)

The victims were tied on a tree before they were executed. The lifeless bodies of the victims were discovered in the Ezo chiefdom on 8 October. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC33734)

desc: On 9 October 2024, Seleka abducted 2 civilians, including the wife of the chief of Zelu groupement in the villages of Dikuma (Ezo, Ango, Bas-Uele) and Mugalie (coded separately). The victims were released a few minutes later. They then captured the nurse in charge of the local health center in Mugalie. 2 FARDC (assumed unarmed) intervened and secured their release. actor1: New Zaire
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-) Republican Guard
title: On 19 May 2024, about 40 armed men led by UCP leader and former FARDC capitaine Christian Malanga drove by Vital Kamerhe's residence (Kamerhe is the p
desc: On 19 May 2024, about 40 armed men led by UCP leader and former FARDC capitaine Christian Malanga drove by Vital Kamerhe's residence (Kamerhe is the presidential candidate for the presidency of the National Assembly), clashing against the policemen assigned to his security and killing two, before storming the Palais de la Nation and flying the flag of 'New Zaire', Malanga's proclaimed new Congolese government, In Kinshasa - Gombe (Kinshasa). Malanga said: 'The time has come, long live Zaire, long live the children of Mobutu. Felix has fallen (...) we are victors'. A clash against the Republican Guard ensued during which Malanga was killed, and about 40 people arrested. Another assailant was killed at Kamerhe's residence. They initially targeted Judith Suminwa (the Prime Minister) and Jean-Pierre Bemba (Minister of Defense) but unable to find them they redirected to Kamerhe's residence. They used a drone to locate their targets. Assailants included two US civilians with no family connection to DRC, a UK civilian, and Congolese from the diaspora and within DRC. The DRC government called the event a coup attempt. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC32638)

desc: On 18 May 2024, rioters burned alive a man in his twenties in Gungu town (Gungu, Kwilu). Population accused him of being a sorcerer behind a series of lighting strikes that regularly kill people in this area. The lynching occurred a day after a lightning strike killed a woman. The man died. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC32700)

desc: On 3 May 2024, M23 fired a bomb at the Lushagala Camp in Goma - Mugunga (Goma, Nord-Kivu). Amnesty International said this was likely in response to rocket fire from FARDC, which had taken up positions close to IDP camps by the town. One of the rockets hit 8eme CEPAC camp, killing a least 18 civilians (likely displaced persons based on the location) including 15 children (six under 5 years old). 30 wounded civilians (of whom 28 women and children). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC32518)

desc: On 29 January 2024, demonstrators barricaded roads with burning tires and clashed with the police in Kimpese (Bangu, Kongo-Central) during a spontaneous demonstration to denounce the state of insecurity in the city following the repetition of attacks perpetrated by unidentified armed men. Rioters looted and set fire to a police station. Police used tear gas and live ammunition. 3 demonstrators were killed and several wounded. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC31735)

desc: On 25 January 2024, M23 overtook control of Mweso following clashes against Wazalendo supported by FARDC (Bashali, Masisi, Nord-Kivu). FARDC fired several rounds of heavy artillery and mistakenly many fell onto populated areas such as Himbi and Kadaf neighborhoods, killing 19 civilians and injuring at least 11 more. A massive displacement of civilians was also reported. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC31594)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)

desc: On 25 January 2024, FARDC and M23 exchanged rocket fire, which landed in Bukumu, Kadaf and Himbi neighborhoods of Mweso (Bashali, Masisi, Nord-Kivu). At least 19 people were killed and 25 others (including 15 children) injured. The house where the artillery had landed was also destroyed. The victims were all civilians who had sought shelter in the targeted house. Both sides blamed the other for civilian casualties. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC33193)

desc: On 15 July 2023, M23 killed about 11-12 hostages (young men) on the Katiro-Birambizo road (on Rubona hill), in the village of Birambizo (Rutshuru, Nord-Kivu). Some of the victims were shot to death and others killed with bladed weapons. The M23 denied the accusations and claimed that the young people killed were not civilians but Wazalendo militia, fighting against each other for the control of the area. MONUSCO announced the deployment of a verification mission to investigate on the killing. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC30226)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: On 12 June 2023, CODECO militiamen attacked an IDP camp hosting mostly Hema displaced people, located in Lala, in the Bahema-Badjere chiefdom, 4km awa
desc: On 12 June 2023, CODECO militiamen attacked an IDP camp hosting mostly Hema displaced people, located in Lala, in the Bahema-Badjere chiefdom, 4km away from Bule (Djugu, Ituri). At least 46 civilians (including 23 minors (25 according to the Congolese authorities) and at least 13 women) were killed. Some were shot dead, some were hacked to death and others were burned alive in their homes. About a dozen civilians were left with serious injuries. Houses were burnt down (according to UNICEF 800 shelters were set on fire) and property and livestock were looted. FARDC and MONUSCO deployed in the nearby town of Bule failed to intervene, even though civilians alerted them while the attack was unfolding. A MONUSCO officer said they did not intervene as one of their armored personnel carriers broke down. The Governor of Ituri claimed armed men were hiding in the camp amongst the IDPs. An investigation was opened and two CODECO leaders were issued arrest warrants by the FARDC. The Ituri Governor claimed that the attack was planned during a meeting held in Dala during which FARDC military outfits were handed over to the militiamen. 14 people suspected to be involved in the attack were arrested on 16 June 2023. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29855)

actor1: Rioters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 12 June 2023, a group of residents demonstrated in Basankusu (Basankusu, Equateur), against the failure of a primate conservation organization to r
desc: On 12 June 2023, a group of residents demonstrated in Basankusu (Basankusu, Equateur), against the failure of a primate conservation organization to respect social clauses signed with the population, and failure to pay customary royalties. The demonstrators killed four primates and set fire to houses on the conservation sites, before marching down to the commercial and administrative center of Basankusu. The police shot in the air to disperse the demonstrators, and calm was restored in the following hours. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29907)

desc: On 18 May 2023, police shot dead two inmates of the Matadi central prison the Matadi high court (Matadi, Kongo-Central), as the detainees were trying to escape. Three detainees successfully escaped and seven were recaptured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29643)

desc: On 16 May 2023, Mobondo clashed against police in Salapamba, a village in Batata territory, coded to territorial capital Bagata (Kwango-Kasai, Bagata, Kwilu). Approximately ten assailants were killed and one police officer severely injured. Police captured six assailants. Mobondo retreated towards Falio village in Kwamouth territory. At least 10 fatalities. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29630)

desc: Around 15 May 2023 (between 13 - 16 May), residents from Osangu and Okasa villages fought one another between Osangu and Okasa villages (Kibombo, Maniema), due to a land tenure conflict between the two villages. Houses were set on fire during the riot. Police and military forces intervened, shot and killed 2-3 persons. One security force was injured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29634)

desc: On 13 May 2023, Mobondo clashed against FARDC in Mongata (Bukanga-Lonzo, Kenge, Kwilu). 11-31 fatalities. The militiamen sexually assaulted at least 11 minors in Mongata and Batshongo. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29629)

desc: On 11 May 2023, Mobondo Yaka ethnic milita attacked and fought with FARDC in Nguma locality, coded to Pont Kwango (Bukanza-Lonzo, Kenge, Kwango). The attack left 1 FARDC and 4-5 militiamen dead. The FARDC also recovered 4 arms and ammunitions. Several Mobondo arrested. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29773)

desc: On 7 May 2023, an unidentified armed group attacked the village of Yosso in Kinshasa (Kinshasa). 9 people were killed in the attack including two children. It is alleged that the killings are part of a land dispute that has been raging since June between the Teke and the Yaka tribes. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29677)

title: On 6 May 2023, police forces clashed with an unidentified armed group people around bloc 3Z in Kasuku (Kailo, Maniema). One fighter belonging to the g
desc: On 6 May 2023, police forces clashed with an unidentified armed group people around bloc 3Z in Kasuku (Kailo, Maniema). One fighter belonging to the group was killed on spot, and the other died of his injuries the next day. A police officer was also injured in the clash. It is alleged that the young people are residents who have been victims of insecurity for several days and were trying to ensure the security of their environment themselves, however the police spokesperson claimed that they were militiamen who wanted to cause an insurrection. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29667)

desc: On 5 May 2023, CODECO and the FPAC Zaire ethnic militia clashed for three consecutive days (coded separately) in the village of Afoyorwot near Yilo (Angal, Mahagi, Ituri). Thirty-eight people were killed in the clashes, including 25 CODECO militiamen, 11 from Zaire and three civilians. Fatalities split across the three events, coded to 12. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29577)

title: On 3 May 2023, armed men assimilated to CMC-Nyatura raided an IDP camp in Kizimba (Bwito, Rutshuru, Nord-Kivu), a locality under M23 control. A clash
desc: On 3 May 2023, armed men assimilated to CMC-Nyatura raided an IDP camp in Kizimba (Bwito, Rutshuru, Nord-Kivu), a locality under M23 control. A clash between CMC-Nyatura and M23 ensued, during which between 13 and 15 IDPs were killed. Five others were seriously injured in the attack and were taken to Mwesso hospital. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29598)

desc: On 2 May 2023, suspected Mbole youths armed with various weapons attacked the Lengola Batiayoke people in the Ngenengene area of Lubunga (Kisangani, Tshopo). The police later recovered three bodies (suspected to have been killed by the armed group), and the body of one of the assailants caught and lynched by the Lengola people (coded separately). The attack signaled a renewal of clashes between the two groups that had locked horns over land ownership. About 40 people were killed in the clash. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29599)

desc: Around 27 April 2023 (between 27 - 28 April), ADF killed around 9 civilians, including about 7 men and 2 women, in Kanaombi and Tengi, Bangole groupement (coded to Mambasa, Mambasa, Ituri). The bodies were discovered on 28 April and buried the next day in Tengi. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC42369)

desc: Around 23 April 2023 (between 19 - 29 April), FARDC, supported by MONUSCO, clashed with ADF in Apetinasana (Oicha, Beni, Nord-Kivu). Conflicting reports mentioned 20-22 ADF fighters were killed in the clash. Four AK-47s were seized by the FARDC. The FARDC also chased the ADF and regained control of the Luna-Komanda road. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29582)

desc: On 17 April 2023, rioters looted and set fire to eight houses in the Bolikango area of Lisala town (Lisala, Mongala). The rioters were youth from a group called 'Base Montagne' who were 'avenging' their peer who died that day from injuries sustained the previous day during a soccer match against another group of youth called 'Base Zaire'. Security forces (police and FARDC) shot in the air to disperse the rioters. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29497)

desc: On 14 April 2023, CODECO killed 44-45 civilians (according to latest reports and MONUSCO confirmation, earlier estimates lower) in Kilo (Baniari de Kilo, Djugu, Ituri). They also set houses and shops on fire, looted goods and destroyed a health center. 19 children were among the victims. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29402)

desc: On 13 April 2023, Mbole community members attacked two Lengola community members over an ongoing land dispute in Lubunga (Kisangani, Tshopo). A group of Lengola then pursed some Mbole. Two people died in the riot. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29421)

They also set fire to a truck, at least 5 motorbikes and bikes. The truck driver was burned but survived. Most of the victims were killed in their fields. IS claimed the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29399)

desc: On 4 April 2023, an unidentified armed men killed a teacher in Pont Kwango (Bukanza-Lonzo, Kenge, Kwango), over an unknown reason, in a context of rising insecurity in the DRC western provinces. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29350)

desc: On 1 April 2023, overnight, rioters burned alive a man they accused of sorcery in Nyaki, 15 kilometers from Gbadolite (Gbadolite, Nord-Ubangi). They accused the man of being part of a group of sorcerers who were behind the death of a youth in the same village two weeks prior. The man died. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29362)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo

desc: On 31 March 2023, CODECO attacked civilians at the Wala mining site and five other nearby villages (Lingbandja, Nyatsa, Ndaki, Ngagodjo, and Katsu), all located in Mambisa chiefdom, coded to chiefdom seat Nizi (Mambisa, Djugu, Ituri). They killed 12 people, including three children, two women, a FARDC (assumed off-duty), and gold miners. They also looted goods (including gold at the mine) and set houses on fire. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29347)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: On 26 March 2023, CODECO killed 17 civilians in Petsi (Walendu-Djatsi, Djugu, Ituri), burning them alive. They had abducted the victims the day prior,
desc: On 26 March 2023, CODECO killed 17 civilians in Petsi (Walendu-Djatsi, Djugu, Ituri), burning them alive. They had abducted the victims the day prior, near Bambu (coded separately). Local reports say the killing was a reprisal after they learned three of their peers were killed in a Zaire ambush on the night of 24 to 25 March (coded separately). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29292)

actor1: Unidentified Armed Group (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 25 March 2023, an unidentified armed group coming from Ngweme farm in Kwamouth territory (Mai-Ndombe province) attacked Teke civilians in Kisia gro
desc: On 25 March 2023, an unidentified armed group coming from Ngweme farm in Kwamouth territory (Mai-Ndombe province) attacked Teke civilians in Kisia groupement, Bagata territory, coded to territorial capital Bagata (Kwango-Kasai, Bagata, Kwilu). The assailants killed 13 to 18 people, including a local Teke chief (whom they decapitated). Others were injured and transferred to the Kikongo hospital. Residents fled towards Fatundu. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29301)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 25 March 2023, the FARDC-UPDF coalition clashed against ADF in the Ruwenzori area (coded to Ruwenzori II, Ruwenzori, Beni, Nord-Kivu), alongside th
desc: On 25 March 2023, the FARDC-UPDF coalition clashed against ADF in the Ruwenzori area (coded to Ruwenzori II, Ruwenzori, Beni, Nord-Kivu), alongside the Nzelube-Lusilubi river, and regained control of the area. FARDC killed 20 ADF (including children), and seized 4 AK-47s and some munitions, as well as 11 Quran, and 7 FARDC outfits. ADF fled towards Ngandu/Virunga National Park. One UPDF was killed. 21 fatalities. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29307)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)

desc: Around 24 March 2023 (between 24 - 25 March), FARDC-UPDF, in a joint military operation, clashed with ADF in a 'surprise attack' on the ADF camp in the thick forests South West of River Lusulubi near River Semuliki-Rwenzori Sector in Loselose (Ruwenzori, Beni, Nord-Kivu). 22 ADF and 1 FARDC-UPDF were killed. No further details. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC42194)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-) Military Police
title: On 23 March 2023, FARDC shot at a gathering of UNAFEC youth (JUNAFEC) in the Kilobelobe neighborhood of Lubumbashi (Lubumbashi, Haut-Katanga). UNAFEC
desc: On 23 March 2023, FARDC shot at a gathering of UNAFEC youth (JUNAFEC) in the Kilobelobe neighborhood of Lubumbashi (Lubumbashi, Haut-Katanga). UNAFEC is a political party that rivals ruling party UPDS. The gathering was the weekly JUNAFEC meeting. Military officers allegedly shot at the crowd and threw several people in the Naviundu river while others jumped into the water to escape. Other sources mention the youth were harassing passerby and threw stones to the military officers. UNAFEC and civil society said 19-45 youth were killed (either shot or drowning to their death). The provincial security council investigated and said 8 had died and two were injured. 8-45 fatalities, coded as 8. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29294)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: On 18 March 2023, CODECO attacked several villages around Ngote (Pandoro, Mahagi, Ituri), including Awuu, Jupazanga, Sana-Ali, Merber, and Teruthoro.
desc: On 18 March 2023, CODECO attacked several villages around Ngote (Pandoro, Mahagi, Ituri), including Awuu, Jupazanga, Sana-Ali, Merber, and Teruthoro. The attackers killed 27-39 civilians (including women and children) with machetes, injured 22 people, and looted and torched houses. Villagers fled. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29198)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: Around 15 March 2023 (month of), ADF killed abducted and killed 13 people including three women around Tenambo (Beni, Nord-Kivu). The victim's corpses
desc: Around 15 March 2023 (month of), ADF killed abducted and killed 13 people including three women around Tenambo (Beni, Nord-Kivu). The victim's corpses were discovered by the FARDC in Tenambo (Beni, Nord-Kivu) three months later. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29945)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 14 March 2023, ADF killed at least 20 civilians in Mabuku village and surrounding settlements (including Mabinda), in Malio grouping (Bashu, Beni,
desc: On 14 March 2023, ADF killed at least 20 civilians in Mabuku village and surrounding settlements (including Mabinda), in Malio grouping (Bashu, Beni, Nord-Kivu), using axes and machetes. IS claimed the attack, saying it killed 'Christians'. Victims included a priest and a village chief. The church was also burned. An unknown number of civilians were abducted. A large number of residents fled the village towards Butembo and Beni. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29112)

actor1: Unidentified Armed Group (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 14 March 2023, unidentified armed men attacked a barge sailing up the Congo river, not far from Mase (Plateaux, Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe). The boat had
desc: On 14 March 2023, unidentified armed men attacked a barge sailing up the Congo river, not far from Mase (Plateaux, Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe). The boat had left Kinshasa with more than 200 civilians and was going to Equateur province. When the passengers saw the assailants, many jumped in the water. 9 bodies (8 civilians from DRC and one from Congo-Brazzaville) were recovered and 190 are missing. There were initially 22 survivors but a further 55 were rescued in the following days. This occurred in the context of a resurgence of violence in Kwamouth between the Teke and Yaka. At least 9 fatalities. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29143)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo

desc: Around 13 March 2023 (from 9 March - 16 March), CODECO killed approximately 10 civilians in Sayo (Banyali-Kilo, Djugu, Ituri). The victims were from the Hema, Alur and Nyali ethnic groups. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29115)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 12 March 2023, overnight, ADF attacked Kirindera/Kilindera (Bashu, Beni, Nord-Kivu) with guns, knives, and machetes, killing 17-22 civilians and in
desc: On 12 March 2023, overnight, ADF attacked Kirindera/Kilindera (Bashu, Beni, Nord-Kivu) with guns, knives, and machetes, killing 17-22 civilians and injuring 4. ADF torched houses, vehicles, and buildings, including a health center, a government building and a hotel. They abducted at least 10 civilians and looted properties. Villagers fled to Kyondo. IS claimed the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29114)

desc: On 12 March 2023, Gumino militia attacked Musika/Masika in Fizi territory (Fizi, Sud-Kivu), killing 7 people and torching houses. Among the casualties were three children who perished in their burning homes, two men who were shot, and two women who were raped and then killed with machetes. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29169)

title: On 8 March 2023, Yaka Ethnic Militia clashed with police in Kinsele (Plateaux, Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe), killing 3 police officers and at least 12 civili
desc: On 8 March 2023, Yaka Ethnic Militia clashed with police in Kinsele (Plateaux, Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe), killing 3 police officers and at least 12 civilians. They also looted and ransacked the village. Residents fled towards Kinshasa, other villages in Kwamouth, and the forest. The attack took place in the context of Teke-Yaka ethnic violence. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29074)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: Around 8 March 2023 (between 8 - 9 March), ADF attacked civilians in Mukondi (Beni, Nord-Kivu) and Mausa (coded separately). They killed 36-45 people
desc: Around 8 March 2023 (between 8 - 9 March), ADF attacked civilians in Mukondi (Beni, Nord-Kivu) and Mausa (coded separately). They killed 36-45 people (including 9 children), all using machetes. At least 10 people were wounded. They set fire to the village and looted it. When fleeing they fired shots into the air to prevent civilians from following them. FARDC arrived on-site after the fact. Residents fled towards Kalunguta and Maboya. IS claimed the attack. This attack might have been in retaliation following the army's arrests of 22 collaborators (coded separately). 36 fatalities split into 2 locations, coded as 18. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29076)

desc: On 4 March 2023, overnight, Mayi Mayi Liwa attacked civilians in Kanje-Africa village, in the Batumba highlands of Tanganyika province, coded to provincial capital Kalemie (Kalemie, Tanganyika). The attack was said to be a retaliation by leader Liwa following the disappearance of his deputy. The attackers, armed with firearms and arrow, killed 4 people including a baby, and seriously injured nine others. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC29033)

title: On 12 February 2023, CODECO-FDBC attacked civilians in Mongbwalu (Banyali-Kilo, Bordj Badji Mokhtar, Ituri). They first shot dead a phone credit selle
desc: On 12 February 2023, CODECO-FDBC attacked civilians in Mongbwalu (Banyali-Kilo, Bordj Badji Mokhtar, Ituri). They first shot dead a phone credit seller/mobile money changer on the street (and wounded his colleague). They then opened fire on the crowd at Poipo Nongo central market, killing 9 civilians on site. 2 others killed near Musaba mountain while the assailants were retreating. Several injured. 12-13 total fatalities. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC28810)

actor1: CODECO-URDPC: Cooperative for Development of Congo (Union of Revolutionaries for the Defense of the Congolese People)
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 27 January 2023, CODECO-URDPC ambushed a FARDC convoy of several 4X4 vehicles heading towards Njala near Pimbo catholic parish (Djugu, Ituri). A fe
desc: On 27 January 2023, CODECO-URDPC ambushed a FARDC convoy of several 4X4 vehicles heading towards Njala near Pimbo catholic parish (Djugu, Ituri). A few hours later, assailants came back to the location to clash against the remaining FARDC who were guarding the damaged vehicles. According to latest reports, 15-17 FARDC killed, including 2 officers. 7 CODECO militants killed and 9 injured. Assailants fled with an important number of weapons and ammunitions. Road traffic on the national road 27, between Iga Barriere and Mahagi was subsequently cut off. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC28491)

actor1: M23: March 23 Movement
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 26 January 2023, M23 clashed all day long with FARDC and Mayi Mayi (unspecified), using light and heavy weaponry (mortars 120 and 82), and overtook
desc: On 26 January 2023, M23 clashed all day long with FARDC and Mayi Mayi (unspecified), using light and heavy weaponry (mortars 120 and 82), and overtook territory in Kitchanga (Bwito, Rutshuru, Nord-Kivu). M23 started to build their own camps, settled in the abandoned FARDC camp as well as in the administrative offices of the city. FARDC claimed they withdrew to avoid civilian fatalities. Veranda Mutsanga civil society organization mentioned instead ammunitions and human resources shortages. They retreated further North in Mweso. M23 leadership claimed they conquered the location to avoid a 'genocide' of the Congolese Tutsis who did not flee the area on 7 December 2022 (coded separately). Some claimed they saw mercenaries (Actualite). According to the DRC government, M23 was support by Rwandese forces equipped with anti-aerial means. Locals fled North of Kitchanga towards Mweso, Kashuga, Kalembe and sheltered in and around MONUSCO base in Kitchanga (coded separately). At least 3 civilians wounded. Heavy losses reported in M23 ranks as well as unknown number of civilian deaths, coded as 10. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC28458)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 22 January 2023, overnight, ADF coming from the Mwalika valley attacked civilians in Makungwe (also known as Vukungwe) (Bashu, Beni, Nord-Kivu). Co
desc: On 22 January 2023, overnight, ADF coming from the Mwalika valley attacked civilians in Makungwe (also known as Vukungwe) (Bashu, Beni, Nord-Kivu). Conflicting reports mentioned ADF killed between 23-29 people, some who were shot, while others were killed with machetes before being burned. Among the victims, 17 were drinking beer at a bar, including 6 women. Six other people had found shelter in a house and were all executed. According to another source, an explosion during the attack killed 17 people. Another source mentioned 13 children between 12-14 years-old were abducted, including two girls. 11 shops (including pharmacies) and 9 houses were set on fire. FARDC were stationed 3 kilometers away and arrived after the assailants had fled towards Graben and Kaviri. There were at least 23 fatalities. IS claimed the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC28418)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)

desc: On 21 January 2023, FARDC clashed with ADF in Mavovo (Ruwenzori, Beni, Nord-Kivu). Fifteen were killed, including 13 FARDC members and two ADF members. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC42127)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 15 January 2023, a hand-made explosive device planted by ADF killed at least 15 civilians (according to latest MONUSCO figures), including women an
desc: On 15 January 2023, a hand-made explosive device planted by ADF killed at least 15 civilians (according to latest MONUSCO figures), including women and at least 5 children, in a CEPAC church (Pentecost) in Kasindi (Ruwenzori, Beni, Nord-Kivu). Over 75 persons injured (six of whom were evacuated by MONUSCO from Beni to Goma for intensive care). One suspect of Kenyan nationality, injured by the blast, arrested. The suspect then escaped. According to FARDC, two more explosive devices remained staged in Kasindi after the explosion. FARDC undertook further investigation to secure the city. IS claimed the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC28297)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: On 13 January 2023, CODECO coming from Mont Bleu and equipped with bladed weapons killed at least 24 civilians, including fishers, in Nyamamba village
desc: On 13 January 2023, CODECO coming from Mont Bleu and equipped with bladed weapons killed at least 24 civilians, including fishers, in Nyamamba village (Bahema Banywagi, Djugu, Ituri). 3 mass graves were discovered on site by MONUSCO (coded separately). CODECO also set fire to houses in the area. Other civilians disappeared. Civilians fled towards Tchomia, Joo and Uganda, across the Albert lake. At least 24 fatalities, including at least 8 women and two children. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC28271)

desc: On 30 December 2022, CODECO attacked civilians in Afoyorwoth, coded to Yilo (Angal, Mahagi, Ituri). The group set on fire the Yilo health center, a school and houses. One civilian and one FARDC (assume off duty) were killed. Civilians, including health workers and patients of the health center, displaced to Ndo. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC27760)

actor1: Twirwaneho Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 29 December 2022, for the second consecutive day, Twirwaneho/Gumino clashed against FARDC, joined by Burundian forces of the joint EAC force, near
desc: On 29 December 2022, for the second consecutive day, Twirwaneho/Gumino clashed against FARDC, joined by Burundian forces of the joint EAC force, near Minembwe (Lulenge, Fizi, Sud-Kivu), precisely in a location called 'UGEAFI', near the local FARDC headquarters. The assailants aimed to attack FARDC headquarters, seize weapons and ammunitions, and take Minembwe town and Kiziba airfield. The clash involved heavy weaponry and machine-guns. 7 FARDC and 4 assailants killed (as per latest report of SOS Medias). 16 wounded, including several children. FARDC succeeded in pushing the assailants back. Hundreds of civilians fled away. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC27741)

desc: On 29 December 2022, a coalition of Hutu-based armed groups (CMC and FDP Nyatura) clashed against M23 in Tongo groupement (Rutshuru, Rutshuru, Nord-Kivu), in a zone where FARDC are not present. The CMC/FDP commander said the coalition killed 13 M23 while 3 coalition fighters were injured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC27951)

desc: On 28 December 2022, CODECO attacked Usigo (Alur-Djuganda, Mahagi, Ituri) in spite of FARDC's deployment of forces in Angal groupement the previous day (coded separately). No intervention of FARDC. 12 civilians were killed (according to latest reports), 3 seriously injured and several houses were set on fire. Assailants also looted goods before fleeing away. A dozen families fled towards Afayuru village. Reports mention CODECO wanted to avenge the murder of three Lendu civilians (their ethnic community), killed by FPAC Zaire (coded separately); violence between the two groups has sharply increased in December. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC27897)

desc: On 26 December 2022, overnight, Mayi Mayi (faction unspecified) clashed with FARDC whilst abducting 5 civilians, including 4 Chinese, all employees of a Chinese gold exploitation company, in Crispin (Babuyu, Kabambare, Maniema). One FARDC died and assailants looted 3 weapons and ammunitions. Assailants requested a 30 000 dollars ransom for the liberation of the abductees. Victims were released on 28 December 2022 after payment of a ransom (amount unspecified). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC28047)

desc: Around 20 December 2022 (as reported), FARDC clashed with an unidentified ethnic militia in Bitadilsawa, within Kenge territory (Kenge, Kwango) to dislodge the unidentified ethnic militia who had 'besieged' the city (coded separately). FARDC regained control of the city. Following their operations, 6 men were arrested, 8 rifles (caliber 12), 15 bullets (caliber 12) and 15 machetes, as well as an important quantity of hemp. FARDC then encouraged locals to return to their homes. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC28100)

actor1: FPAC: Ituri Self-Defense Popular Front (Zaire)

desc: On 19 December 2022, FPAC/Zaire attacked civilians in Azimini (also spelled as Zimini; Dzi) at the border of Godawasi (also known as Godawisi) (Walendu-Watsi, Mahagi, Ituri). 9 civilians were killed including an old woman who got shot, and 8 children hacked to death. 3 civilians were seriously injured during the incursion. 2 others disappeared. Zaire set 100 of houses on fire and looted goats and livestock. This attack unfolded after several retaliation attacks of both Zaire and CODECO over the course of a week in Mahagi territory. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC27999)

actor1: M23: March 23 Movement
actor2: FDLR: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda
title: On 29 November 2022, M23 clashed with FDLR and a coalition of Mayi Mayi including Nyatura (Hutu Based), CMC and Mazembe in Kishishi (also spelt as Kis
desc: On 29 November 2022, M23 clashed with FDLR and a coalition of Mayi Mayi including Nyatura (Hutu Based), CMC and Mazembe in Kishishi (also spelt as Kishishe) - and surrounding locations such as Bambo, Bunyama, Kilama, and Kongakonga - a locality of Bambu groupement (Bwito, Rutshuru, Nord-Kivu). M23 regained control of Kishishe, lost a few days earlier (coded separately). During and after the clashes, 131-272 civilians were killed, including IDPs and children, by the M23 over suspicion of alliance with the Mayi Mayi. A local dignitary said 64 victims had taken refuge in a church and all men had been shot whilst women were released. 20 men, mostly young, were abducted by the group and brought to their position in Rubare. The DRC government, who claimed 272 civilians were killed, including at least 17 children, called for an international investigation for war crimes. M23, who called for an 'independent inquiry', rejected the allegations and instead claimed that 21 Mayi Mayi were killed during the clashes and 8 civilians were killed by stray bullets. An investigation was opened by the Attorney General. The UN deployed a preliminary investigation which concluded on 8 December 2022 in at least 131 civilians killed by bullets and bladed weapons between 29 and 30 November 2022. The report also mentioned the nearby Bambu village as having been affected with 60 civilians abducted and 22 women and 5 girls raped. The M23 rejected the conclusions of the UN investigation claiming it was not impartial. Likewise, on 21 December 2022, the Rwandese Government accused DRC of having 'fabricated' conclusions without a serious entity conducting investigations on the events in order to put the blame on the M23. In February 2023, Human Rights Watch said they were able to verify the identity of 22 civilians killed. Also in February 2023, Amnesty International said M23 had killed at least 20 civilians, and raped 66 women and girls in the area from 21-30 November (some specific instances coded). M23 rejected the sexual violence accusations. Civilians fled towards Kibirizi. Witnesses mentioned they saw mass graves and some victims recounted they had to bury themselves victims. On 14 June 2023, HRW reported on the presence of 14 mass graves in Kishishe (coded separately). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC27624)

desc: On 9 October 2022, unidentified ethnic militia attacked Kimpana Mwanango (Wamba - Fatundu, Bagata, Kwilu) where they killed 2 men and a woman, abducted 2 civilians and burnt more than 20 houses. According to the civil society, no police officers were present at the local police station. Thugs also seized the opportunity to loot crops and farms. The event happened in the context of ethnic conflicts between Teke and Yaka in the region. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC27111)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces

desc: On 4 October 2022, ADF clashed against FARDC and UPDF in Vido I (Beni, Beni, Nord-Kivu). The joint military forces intervened as ADF was attacking civilians. Before the arrival of the soldiers ADF killed 11 civilians using bladed weapons, including three women and an Anglican Church pastor, and set more than 25 houses on fire. At least 20 people, including a woman, disappeared. Casualties of the clash unknown; at least 11 fatalities total. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26991)

desc: On 2 October 2022, ADF clashed with FARDC as the former were attacking civilians in Beu Manyama/Kota Okola village, Mamove locality (Oicha, Beni, Nord-Kivu). ADF killed 17 civilians and 3 FARDC. ADF also burned a dozen houses. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26994)

desc: On 2 October 2022, a FARDC soldier beat a young man who refused to transport hemp on his behalf in Tumba-Sumbula (Kasai-Kabambaie, Kamonia, Kasai). The young man died two days later. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC27033)

ADF also abducted a dozen people and set fire to 36 houses. 200 households fled. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26992)

desc: On 23 September 2022, an unidentified communal militia armed with machetes, arrows, and guns killed about 90 people, including several teachers and students, in the village of Bishala (Plateaux, Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe), in the context of intercommunal conflicts. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26963)

desc: On 20 September 2022, Teke and Yaka ethnic militias clashed against one another in Ingwene and Bisiala villages, Kwamouth territory, coded to territorial capital Kwamouth (Plateaux, Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe). Fatality estimates range from 64 to 90, with 25 Teke (including the customary chief), 45 Yaka and 5 FARDC dead in Ingwene, and approximately 20 people killed in Bisiala. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26938)

desc: On 20 September 2022, FARDC and FDNB clashed against FNL - Nzabampema in Namaramara locality, near Masango (Itombwe, Mwenga, Sud-Kivu). FNL said they killed around 20 FARDC/FDNB who were coming to attack them from Masango and Maheta; and captured a FDNB caporal and that three FNL were injured. More than 3000 people fled Namaramara. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26908)

desc: On 20 September 2022, ADF and their Mayi Mayi allies attacked civilians on the Bunia-Kisangani road at Bandikindo between Kindalakundala and Tolitoli (Mambasa, Ituri). ADF killed at least 10 civilians, including a Somali truck driver and six other truck drivers, and abducted at least another 10 civilians. They also set fire to 7 vehicles, including 5 petrol trucks. When FARDC got to the scene it was too late. Residents from Mugamba and Lolwa villages, near Tolitoli, fled towards Komanda. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26907)

desc: On 19 September 2022, an unidentified communal militia attacked civilians in Mbomo (Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe), in the context of the ongoing Teke/Yaka conflict. At least 7 people were killed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26960)

desc: On 12 September 2022, overnight, Teke and Yaka ethnic militias clashed against each other in Fadiaka (Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe) alongside the National Road 17. 30-35 people killed, including the village chief who was beheaded, and tens of houses were burnt. An unidentified number of people were arrested; amongst the arrested suspects, some were wearing military uniforms. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26881)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo

desc: On 9 September 2022, CODECO attacked civilians with guns and knives in Mbidjo (Djugu, Ituri), killing at least 40 peoples including 15 women and 17 children, and burning 400 houses alongside with some shops and motorbikes. The victims were killed either in their burning houses or by gunshots. Assailants looted goods. Population fled towards the forest and nearby Pluto and Nzebi mine sites. 18 500 people were displaced towards Dala and Mongwalu localities. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26654)

desc: On 6 September 2022, Teke militia, arriving from 4 different villages (Camp Banku, Bisiala, Menko), clashed with Yaka ethnic militia on the national road 17 in Mibe (Plateaux, Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe). 7-14 Yaka killed and 2-4 Teke killed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26642)

desc: Around 2 September 2022, Twas militiamen killed one civilian and one unarmed FARDC in Mapanda (Kalemie, Tanganyika). The victims were ambushed on the road between Kalemie and Bendera. The driver of the motorbike was gunshot whilst the military officer was killed by machete in the bush. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26692)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 31 August 2022, during a two-day battle between CODECO and Congolese security forces in Mongbwalu (Banyali-Kilo, Bordj Badji Mokhtar, Ituri), CODEC
desc: On 31 August 2022, during a two-day battle between CODECO and Congolese security forces in Mongbwalu (Banyali-Kilo, Bordj Badji Mokhtar, Ituri), CODECO burnt 60 houses and shops at Kilima Mungu mine camp located within the city. The objective of CODECO was to free some of their combatants at the FARDC headquarter. At least 33 fatalities, including at least 14 civilians and at least 8 CODECO members, split across two days. 4 civilians injured. One police officer was injured by gunshots and a local police station was ransacked. Massive displacement (4000 people) of civilians towards nearby villages and Mongbwalu-center. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26617)

The clash lead to 6 ADF killed and 4 weapons recovered. One girl was left behind by the group. ADF fled towards Kotakoli and committed other killings on their way (coded separately). At least 11 fatalities. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26793)

Civilians fled towards Beni. FARDC did not confirm the death toll. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC27085)

title: On 19 August 2022, Yaka and Teke ethnic militias clashed in Masiakwa (coded to Kwamouth, Plateaux, Mai-Ndombe) as 200 Yaka raided the village. The cla
desc: On 19 August 2022, Yaka and Teke ethnic militias clashed in Masiakwa (coded to Kwamouth, Plateaux, Mai-Ndombe) as 200 Yaka raided the village. The clash resulted in 15 civilians dead, 8 Teke and 7 Yaka; and more than 31 severely wounded. The village chief of Masiakwa and his wife are amongst the victims; they have been beheaded. More than 80 houses were burned down and a pharmacy, as well as shops, looted. Civilians fled the area towards neighboring Bolobo territory, as well as Brazzaville and Kinshasa. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26841)

desc: On 18 August 2022, and for the second day, Yaka ethnic militants clashed with Teke ethnic militants in Miboro, near Gambomi (Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe). 7 Yaka died, 4 people injured and several houses were burnt. 15 children whose parents have fled during the clash were seen roaming around. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26840)

desc: On 17 August 2022, the Gumino-Twirwaneho-Android coalition clashed against the mayi mayi Biloze Bishambuke at a mine in Bigaragara (Itombwe, Mwenga, Sud-Kivu), over the exploitation of the mine. 414 houses were set on fire. 21 civilians were killed (nine women, six children and six men), including eight found charred in their homes. 14 injured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26797)

desc: On 16 August 2022, CODECO clashed against FARDC in Waya village, near Mabanga (Mambisa, Djugu, Ituri). They first attacked civilians, killing them with machetes and firearms, before FARDC intervened. They also looted mining equipment and previous minerals. They retreated to their Bakombe bastion following FARDC intervention. 8 to 17 civilians and 2 to 4 FARDC were killed, 2 were civilians wounded. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26724)

desc: On 14 August 2022, FARDC killed 10 ADF in Malaya (Bahema-Boga, Irumu, Ituri), after the ADF attacked the locality and nearby Malibongo (coded separately). FARDC captured 6 ADF, including two children under 10 of Congolese and Ugandan nationality, and seized AK-47s and IEDs. Local residents fled towards Kinyanjojo. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26790)

Police shot live ammunition at the rioters, killing 2 and wounding several others. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26532)

desc: On 26 July 2022, rioters gathered in front of MONUSCO base in Butembo (Nord-Kivu) to demonstrate against the presence of the UN mission in the country. Heavy deployment of security forces (assumed police and military) used tear gas to disperse the crowd and prevent any intrusion within the base. 9 civilians were killed. 4 UN security officers were killed with weapons seized from police. A report mentions a blue helmet was accidentally shot by another UN police officer. FARDC accused the Mayi Mayi Militia (Kyandenga) of organizing and supporting the attack against MONUSCO. One UN security force soldier was seriously injured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26330)

At least 40 houses burnt. Locals fled towards safer areas. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26686)

desc: On 19 July 2022, police killed a civilian and wounded another, associated with the Kuluna, in Kinshasa - Ngaliema (Kinshasa, Kinshasa). This occurred following an altercation between a tenant and her landlord. The Kuluna and police got involved; police shot at the tenant, killing her. The Kuluna then threw stones at the police, who fired another shot and injured one of them. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26290)

desc: On 17 July 2022, Bena Muembia alongside Bena Kapuya clashed with Bena Nshimba militiamen in Bakua Tshitongo, 60 km from Mbuji Mayi (Mbuji-Mayi, Kasai-Oriental) over a land dispute. However, according to local authorities, the clash started over the perception that a Bena Nshimba funeral precession was assailants who wanted to attack Bena Kapuya. 3 Bena Nshimba militants killed by machetes and 1 died in the aftermath of two days of clashes (first day coded separately). 4 fatalities split into two events: 2 on 17 July and 2 fatalities coded the previous day. 47 houses burnt. Civilians fled in Katanda. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26218)

actor1: Mayi Mayi Militia (Yira)
title: On 7 July 2022, armed men attacked a health center linked with a church in Lume (Ruwenzori, Beni, Nord-Kivu), setting four of its buildings on fire al
desc: On 7 July 2022, armed men attacked a health center linked with a church in Lume (Ruwenzori, Beni, Nord-Kivu), setting four of its buildings on fire alongside with 5 residential houses in the Mwangaza neighborhood. 11-15 people were killed, including a nurse and three patients who were burned alive in the health center, as well as three children and 4 people were killed by machete and guns in the Kidolo area of Lume. 4 civilians wounded. Other civilians were abducted. The attackers also looted medicine. The attack was originally attributed to the ADF before the army said it was Mayi Mayi Yira, who might have been working on behalf of the ADF. IS claimed the attack. At the end of November 2022, at least 2 of the hostages were freed by FARDC operations (coded separately). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC26027)

desc: On 21 November 2021, the CODECO-URDPC attacked a refugee site and took over the village of Drodro (Djugu, Ituri), where they also ransacked the Catholic church, stole goods from shops, and looted cows. The next day, the FARDC regained control of this village during clashes (coded as another event). At least 20 civilians, including a teacher and at least 6 children, were discovered dead (unclear whether the fatalities took place on this day); other sources say 50 people were killed (16 fatalities on 21 November and 21 others on 22 November). 20 fatalities coded and spread across two events. Over 15,500 people were displaced to the locality of Roe. Several people were reported missing. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC23343)

The group claims to be affiliated to the NDC and APCLS (armed groups have not claimed event). Flags of the ACN political party were raised by the group; the ACN later denied being part of this incident. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC23069)

desc: Displacement: Around 18 October 2021 (from 10 October - 27 October), 15,059 refugees from CAR arrived to the territory of Bosobolo (Nord-Ubangi). The refugees were reportedly fleeing clashes between the FACA and Seleka fighters in Kouango. Among the refugees are over 1,637 women and 11,892 children. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC23125)

desc: On 13 October 2021, the communities of the Lingotebe and Ngbanda groupments clashed against one another over a land conflict in the territory of Gemena (Sud-Ubangi) following clashes in this territory and in Kungu on 11-12 October. In this territory, at least 3 people died (8-13 fatalities in both territories). Security forces were deployed to control the fighting between these communities on this day. 3 fatalities coded across two events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22814)

The FARDC restored order in Gemena territory on 13 October (coded as another event). 3 fatalities coded across 2 days of fighting. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22804)

desc: On 19 September 2021, a group of people killed a police officer after he killed a woman in the locality of Mpanda, located in the territory of Kabeya-Kamwanga (Kasai-Oriental). The woman was killed after an altercation with the police officer, who almost hit her child while riding a motorcycle. Police officers burnt 8 houses in reprisal for the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22552)

At least 2-3 people were killed, including a FARDC officer and a girl. Other people were injured. Community displacements were triggered. Other sources suggest the attack took place in Crispin village. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22499)

desc: On 23 August 2021, an unidentified armed group attacked an abbot (Catholic Christian) and his driver in a vehicle near the Konzi pond along the Kinshasa - Kikwit route, 30 km of Kenge city (Kenge, Kwango). The armed group injured the abbot, stole the vehicle as well as a large sum of money. Sources suggest that the abbott was on his way to Kikwit to pay health workers. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22288)

desc: Around 21 August 2021 (between 19 - 23 August), the FARDC clashed against the ADF in the village of Katanda/Kathanda/Katanga, located in the chiefdom of Bashu (Beni, Nord-Kivu), repelling an ADF incursion. At least 9-13 civilians were killed by the ADF. Subsequent reports suggest that 8 ADF militants were killed as well as 1 soldier. 46 houses were burnt and civilians were abducted. The FARDC managed to release at least 15 hostages. The ISCAP claimed the event and the killing of 9 Christian civilians. A total of at least 18 fatalities coded. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22253)

desc: On 18 August 2021, Mbororo pastoralists killed 3 people in the locality of Nawiliko, located in the territory of Ango (Bas-Uele). 2 of the victims were shot while the third was decapitated. Another civilian was injured. Subsequent sources suggest the incident took place in the locality of Makpangi, territory of Ango. The assailants were reportedly arrested on 26 August. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22245)

desc: On 17 August 2021, the FARDC regained control of Lisey/Liseyi and Itedey/Itendey, near Kilo (Djugu, Ituri), after carrying out airstrikes against the CODECO-URDPC. In Lisey, the CODECO-URDPC attacked the village's commercial center and its protestant mission. They also burnt the general hospital of Kilo located in Itedey/Intendey and ransacked/burnt homes. Overall, in Lisey and Itendey/Itendey, at least 10 militiamen, 3 FARDC, and a woman civilian were killed, while a child was wounded. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22244)

desc: Around 17 August 2021 (between 16 - 18 August), the FARDC clashed against an unidentified armed group in the Batwa groupment of Kadimba, health zone of Kakenge (Mweka, Kasai). The clashes were triggered by the killing of a FARDC officer and a local chief by elements of the unidentified armed group. Over 20,700 people fled as a result of the clashes (coded as another event). While 2 fatalities were reported (as the triggers of the conflict), any other fatalities that may have taken place through this timeframe are unknown. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22409)

desc: On 14 August 2021, FARDC attacked ADF who killed at least 14 civilians and wounded 9-10 others in the village of Kikingi (Watalinga, Beni, Nord-Kivu). During the clashes, 4 ADF and 3 FARDC were killed. 25 others were abducted. The ADF reportedly destroyed five shops and three pharmacies. Over 9,300 civilians were displaced. One hostage was freed at the end of November 2022 during FARDC operations (coded separately). She mentioned of being raped everyday by her attributed ADF husband during her captivity. According to her, some of the hostages were killed (no specific figures, coded as 3). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22211)

The army reports this was the training bastion of the ADF. They also attacked other nearby areas, including Lusilubi, Nzelube, Semuliki and Talya rivers. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC22156)

The ISCAP claimed the event, reporting 15 fatalities against Christians. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC21945)

title: On 14 June 2021, FARDC supported by MONUSCO retaliated against a CODECO (URDPC) attack in Fataki (Walendu Djatsi, Djugu, Ituri). The FARDC supported b
desc: On 14 June 2021, FARDC supported by MONUSCO retaliated against a CODECO (URDPC) attack in Fataki (Walendu Djatsi, Djugu, Ituri). The FARDC supported by MONUSCO drove the militiamen out of the village shortly after the CODECO (URDPC) drove the FARDC out of the village on the same day. During the clashes on this day, 25 CODECO (URDPC) were killed, 4 FARDC were injured and 2 CODECO (URDPC) were captured by FARDC. Overall, the clashes took place over 5 days (coded separately). Some reports suggest that at least 42 militiamen were killed as well as 2 FARDC soldiers during the 5-day clashes. 8,000 people were displaced as a result of the clashes, the majority of whom sought refuge at the MONUSCO in Djaiba. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC21584)

During the clashes on this day, 17 CODECO (URDPC) and 2 FARDC were killed while 1 FARDC was wounded. FARDC recovered an AK47. Overall, the clashes took place over 5 days (coded separately), and some reports suggest that at least 42 militiamen were killed as well as 2 FARDC soldiers during the 5-day clashes. 8,000 people were displaced as a result of the clashes, and the majority of whom sought refuge at the MONUSCO in Djaiba. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC21577)

desc: Around 9 June 2021 (week of), a group of miners gathered in the village of Kisankala (Lubudi, Lualaba) to protest against the management of the Kisankala mining site and their complicity in allowing the looting of resources in the site during the night time. The FARDC shot and killed 4 to 6 miners who sought to raise these concerns to provincial authorities in Kolwezi. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC21574)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 7 June 2021, the FARDC, along with the MONUSCO, clashed against the ADF in the villages of Kinyanjojo and Boga (Irumu, Ituri). Initially, during th
desc: On 7 June 2021, the FARDC, along with the MONUSCO, clashed against the ADF in the villages of Kinyanjojo and Boga (Irumu, Ituri). Initially, during the attacks, at least 12 people were killed (2 of whom were ADF militants), 3 medical compounds were burnt, several civilians were abducted, 1 FARDC soldier was injured, and one grave was desecrated. 2 AK47 weapons were recovered. On 20 June, at least 23 bodies of civilians, which were reportedly killed on this day, were recovered in Boga and Tshabi (coded as another event). Some sources suggest the attack was carried out by the ADF in coalition with a Hutu-led group, the Banyabwisha. Community displacements were triggered. In total, with the 20 June body discovery, at least 18 fatalities took place. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC21541)

actor2: Unidentified Armed Group (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 31 May 2021, the FARDC clashed against an unidentified armed group in the villages of Rubingo, Kinyanjojo and Boga (Irumu, Ituri). During the incid
desc: On 31 May 2021, the FARDC clashed against an unidentified armed group in the villages of Rubingo, Kinyanjojo and Boga (Irumu, Ituri). During the incident, 28-32 people, including IDPs and 1-2 FARDC soldiers were killed, while 10 other civilians were injured. 15 houses and 6 vehicles were burnt. Another parallel attack by the armed group took place in the village of Tshabi (coded as another event). Altogether, 48000 people were displaced and 27 others were abducted. Some sources speculate the attack was carried out by the ADF, while others say it may have been an ethnic clash between the Nyali and Banyabwisha. 28 fatalities spread across three events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC21477)

desc: On 31 May 2021, the FARDC/MONUSCO clashed against an unidentified armed group in Tshabi (Baniari-Tchabi, Irumu, Ituri), while the said group killed 23-28 people in the village (including 4 women, 15 men, and 4 children) and kidnapped 10 others. They set fire to houses and stole goods. The group also attacked the villages of Boga, Rubingo, and Kinyanjojo (coded separately). Altogether, 48,000 were displaced, and 27 others were abducted. Some sources said that the attack was carried out by the ADF, while others claimed it could have been an ethnic clash between the Nyali and Banyabwisha. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC21484)

desc: On 13 May 2021, two rival Muslim groups of the COMICO clashed against one another at the Martyrs stadium of Kinshasa (Kinshasa, Kinshasa) over a leadership/succession dispute within the organization, which took place by the end of Ramadan. The PNC attempted to contain the situation, dispersing the rioters with tear gas/firing shots in the air. During the clashes, 1 policeman was burnt to death and an older woman later died, along with vehicles vandalized. 40-49 people were injured and 30-35 detained by the PNC. An Imam's residence in Kinshasa was also set ablaze on this day by members of the Muslim community. On 2 July, another policeman, who had been injured on this day, died. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC21320)

desc: Around 28 March 2021 (between 26 March - 30 March), a group of Kuba rioters clashed against the Luba ethnic group in Kakenge (also known as Bakwakenge) (Mweka, Kasai), resulting in the deaths of at least 13 people, 100-190 houses were burned, including the house of a local senator, and a health center looted. Some sources suggest that the clashes were triggered by a disagreement between a man of the Kuba tribe who lived in a free union relationship with a Lubaphone woman. Sources suggest the clashes may have taken place in Mwenge or Muanga. Between 21,000 to 25,000 people were displaced following the clashes. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20744)

desc: On 27 March 2021, the ADF attacked Muziranduru/Musirandura (Beni, Nord-Kivu), where they killed 2 civilians, abducted several people and set on fire 28 houses, including that of the governor's delegated official. On 28 March, 17 hostages were released (including 13 children and 4 women). Some sources suggested that 19 other hostages were still in captivity. Around 16 June, Mulango inhabitants reportedly discovered 17 dead bodies in Muziranduru/Musirandura. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20701)

Subsequent reports suggest that, during the month, the ADF also attacked other villages, including Muntobo, Kakalali, Kalembo, Kainana, Libokora/Libokara, Bunyondo/Baoba, Bulongo, ChanChan and Makisabo (coded as other events). Overall, in these locations, 85 people were killed and 12 people were abducted. Out of the 85 fatalities, around 8 people died in Bulongo. 77 fatalities coded across 7 events. title: On 22 March 2021, the FARDC regained territory in the hills of Dhi and Boza, located in Iga Barriere (Djugu, Ituri), following a two-day clash against
desc: On 22 March 2021, the FARDC regained territory in the hills of Dhi and Boza, located in Iga Barriere (Djugu, Ituri), following a two-day clash against the CODECO-FDBC at a commercial center in the area. 27 militants were killed, including one of the group's head commanders, as well as 1 FARDC soldier. 8 AK47 weapons were recovered. According to reports, the armed clash was triggered by a CODECO/FDBC incursion in the area of Dhi, where they killed 5-12 people and abducted 6 others. They also carried out incursions in Sawe, Baluma, and Nizi 4. During the incursions, a vehicle of the World Food Program was set on fire. 33 fatalities spread across two events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20680)

desc: Around 15 February 2021 (month of), elements of Nkundo/Mongo (Bantu) engaged in an armed clash against Batwa militiamen in or near Bianga, territory of Monkoto (Tshuapa). The armed clashes took place within the context of a succession of conflicts that began in December 2020, when a Batwa was found dead while working in a Bantu field. Subsequent reports suggest that over 66 people were killed during an attack to Iyeke community/Batwa ethnic group over a period of three days, during which at least 66 people were killed. Fatalities spread across 4 events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20333)

desc: On 14 February 2021, Bakata Katanga militants clashed with the FARDC in Lubumbashi (Lubumbashi, Haut-Katanga), following an attempt to loot arms at two military camps, Kimbembe and Kibati. At least 16 people were killed as a result of the clash, including 4 FARDC/Republican Guard. According to reports, about ten people, including elements of the FARDC and Bakata Katanga, were detained and transferred to the Ndolo prison in Kinshasa. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20326)

actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 14 February 2021, ADF militants clashed with elements of the FARDC in Ndalya (Irumu, Ituri) during an attack against civilians at a nearby church.
desc: On 14 February 2021, ADF militants clashed with elements of the FARDC in Ndalya (Irumu, Ituri) during an attack against civilians at a nearby church. About 16 to 24 people, including Christians, died as a result of the armed clash, 11 were wounded, and several others were abducted, including 6 children (4 boys and 2 girls). The armed clashes triggered community displacements. On 18 February, between 12-23 hostages were released in Mambelenga (Irumu, Ituri). Subsequent reports suggest that the ISCAP claimed the attack. On 12 March, the ADF liberated 12 more hostages. Other sources suggest that the hostages were released on 19 February. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20327)

desc: On 11 February 2021, the FARDC engaged in an armed clash against elements of the ADF in the villages of Ntoma and Mabondo (Beni, Nord-Kivu), resulting in the killing of 10 ADF militants. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20395)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 8 February 2021, members of the ADF killed around 11 Nande civilians in the village of Kabembeu (Oicha, Nord-Kivu), 9 km away from Mutwanga in Ruwe
desc: On 8 February 2021, members of the ADF killed around 11 Nande civilians in the village of Kabembeu (Oicha, Nord-Kivu), 9 km away from Mutwanga in Ruwenzori. Subsequent reports suggest that the victims of the attack were farmers seeking refuge in a camp nearby. 3 people were wounded and 3 others abducted. Other sources suggest the attack took place in the village of Mwenda, namely in the Kithobira hills. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20272)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces

desc: On 5 February 2021, the ADF killed at least 16 Nande farmers in Mabule village, located approximately 2km from Kisima (Oicha, Nord-Kivu). Disappearances were reported, but the number is unknown. Subsequent reports suggest that the Islamic State Central Africa claimed the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20251)

actor1: Nkundo Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 2 February 2021, and for the second of three days, hundreds of Nkundo armed individuals killed at least 66 Iyeke indigenous people in eight village
desc: On 2 February 2021, and for the second of three days, hundreds of Nkundo armed individuals killed at least 66 Iyeke indigenous people in eight villages of the Bianga sector (Monkoto, Tshuapa). Among the victims, at least 40 were children; the group set over 1000 houses on fire during the attacks. Other sources suggest that an armed clash between the Nkundo/Mungo (Bantu) and a Batwa was also observed during the month (coded as another event). Fatalities split into 3 events, coded as 22. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC24293)

actor1: Mayi Mayi Militia (Kashumba)
actor2: Twirwaneho Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 26 January 2021, a coalition of Mayi-Mayi Kashumba, Makanaki, Rene and Biloze Bishambuke attacked Twirwaneho and Makanika fighters in the village o
desc: On 26 January 2021, a coalition of Mayi-Mayi Kashumba, Makanaki, Rene and Biloze Bishambuke attacked Twirwaneho and Makanika fighters in the village of Gongwa (Mwenga, Sud-Kivu) and other adjacent villages. 18 Twirwaneho fighters were killed in the attack. The Mayi-Mayi coalition drove off the Twirwaneho/Makanika and set houses on fire. The clash is related to cycles of reprisals over land, ethnic conflicts, and cattle-looting. Fatalities coded across 2 events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20164)

actor1: Gumino Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
