desc: On 25 January 2021, Gumino militiamen and allies clashed with Mayi-Mayi Biloze Bishambuke militia fighters in Ngoma village, near Kitasha (Mwenga, Sud-Kivu). 9 Gumino combatants were killed and others wounded. The clashes forced the locals to flee the area. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20130)

actor1: Twirwaneho Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 24 January 2021, Twirwaneho/Makanika militiamen clashed for hours with a group of Mayi-Mayi Biloze Bishambuke men in Kwisumu village (Mwenga, Sud-K
desc: On 24 January 2021, Twirwaneho/Makanika militiamen clashed for hours with a group of Mayi-Mayi Biloze Bishambuke men in Kwisumu village (Mwenga, Sud-Kivu), following an attack by the Biloze Bishambuke. Overall, 19 Twirwaneho fighters were killed and from the ranks of the Biloze Bishambuke, 4 were killed and 7 were wounded. The clash is related to cycles of reprisals over land, ethnic conflicts, and cattle-looting. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20123)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)

title: On 18 January 2021, the FARDC clashed with a coalition of CODECO, FRPI and FPIC militiamen in Djunde village, near Chini ya Kilima (Irumu, Ituri), fol
desc: On 18 January 2021, the FARDC clashed with a coalition of CODECO, FRPI and FPIC militiamen in Djunde village, near Chini ya Kilima (Irumu, Ituri), following an operation by the army. 13 elements of this coalition were killed and several weapons seized by the army. The rest of these rebels fled this village, where they had established their temporary base. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20056)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
actor2: Batwa Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 16 January 2021, a group of Batwa militiamen armed with firearms, arrows, and machetes clashed with FARDC/PNC elements in Bakwa Kadimba village, 19
desc: On 16 January 2021, a group of Batwa militiamen armed with firearms, arrows, and machetes clashed with FARDC/PNC elements in Bakwa Kadimba village, 195 Km north of Mweka town, and near Dekse (Dekese, Kasai). Between 4 and 6 soldiers were killed, as the militiamen took control of the village. Subsequent sources point out that 5 civilians were killed during the clashes. The next day, on 17 January, Congolese state/security forces arrested and transferred two members of the group to Kananga, including its leader, Katende Tshiombo. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20043)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces

desc: On 13 January 2021, suspected ADF militants killed 16 civilians, including 6 women and 8 children, from the Mbuti community in Avombi village, located in the territory of Irumu (Ituri). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20651)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 13 January 2021, ADF militia rebels killed at least 46-47 civilians (Batwa ethnicity) with machetes (decapitated) and injured 2 others, during an a
desc: On 13 January 2021, ADF militia rebels killed at least 46-47 civilians (Batwa ethnicity) with machetes (decapitated) and injured 2 others, during an attack in Abembi village, near Tshabi (Irumu, Ituri). Many of the victims were women and children. Some reports suggested that some Banyabwisha Hutu groups were behind the attack and not the ADF, as the attack targeted only Batwa people. Authorities sent an investigation team to clarify the facts. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20001)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
actor2: Chini Ya Kilima-FPIC: Patriotic and Integrationist Force of Congo
title: On 7 January 2021, the FARDC and police clashed with FPIC militiamen in Lengabo (Irumu, Ituri), following an operation by the security forces. At leas
desc: On 7 January 2021, the FARDC and police clashed with FPIC militiamen in Lengabo (Irumu, Ituri), following an operation by the security forces. At least 3 FPIC rebels were killed, 1 was seriously injured and 6 others were captured. 1 FARDC soldier and 1 police were also reportedly killed and 4 soldiers injured. The driver of a military truck that was attacked by the FPIC went missing. The bodies of 28 civilians were found days later. They were said to have been tied up and executed by FPIC militiamen. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19692)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 5 January 2021, the FARDC clashed with ADF militia combatants in Loselose village, near Mwenda (Beni, Nord-Kivu), following another military offens
desc: On 5 January 2021, the FARDC clashed with ADF militia combatants in Loselose village, near Mwenda (Beni, Nord-Kivu), following another military offensive after these rebels had occupied the village the previous week. This time the FARDC managed to drive the rebels out of the village. Reportedly, during their flight the ADF killed 50 hostages. Alternate reports suggest that 50 ADF bodies were uncovered at the scene. It is unclear whether these refer to the same bodies. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19673)

desc: On 4 January 2021, ADF militia rebels armed with firearms and machetes killed at least 23 Nande civilians (including 12 women and 11 men) during an attack in the village of Mwenda (Oicha, Nord-Kivu). At least 10 other civilians went missing. The attack is believed to be a response to recent clashes with the FARDC in Loselose village, which ended in dozens of rebels killed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19663)

desc: On 3 January 2021, the ADF carried out an incursion in the village of Lulo, located in the territory of Beni (Beni, Nord-Kivu), which resulted in the killing of 16 civilians. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20772)

desc: On 3 January 2021, 2 communal militias armed with edged weapons clashed with each other in Panu locality (Idiofa, Kwilu), resulting in 2 people killed and more than 10 houses torched. The police were deployed later on to control the situation. The motive behind the clash was not reported. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19655)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 1 January 2021, the FARDC clashed with ADF combatants in Loselose village (Beni, Nord-Kivu), following a new military offensive after these rebels
desc: On 1 January 2021, the FARDC clashed with ADF combatants in Loselose village (Beni, Nord-Kivu), following a new military offensive after these rebels had occupied the village earlier that week. 10 civilians were killed in the clash and 16 ADF rebels and 2 FARDC soldiers were also killed (among the ADF's dead, 2 alleged Arab combatants were identified). At least 7 soldiers were injured. Other sources said that 7 soldiers were killed (not confirmed). The FARDC drove the ADF out of the village. The Islamic State group claimed the attack against the army. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19610)

desc: Around 12 December 2020, ADF militia rebels armed with firearms and machetes killed at least 20 civilians, including women, children and a journalist from a community radio station, during an attack in Nzenga village (Oicha, Nord-Kivu). Other civilians were abducted during the overnight attack. The assailants also looted local shops and burned down some homes. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19602)

desc: On 11 December 2020, ADF militia rebels armed with firearms and machetes killed at least 1 FARDC soldier and 1 woman during an attack in Maitatu village, near Ndalya (Irumu, Ituri). The attackers also injured 2 other civilians and abducted more than 20. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19750)

desc: On 8 December 2020, ADF militiamen armed with machetes and firearms clashed with the FARDC in Ndalya village (Irumu, Ituri), following an attack by these rebels. 1 FARDC soldier and 1 civilian man were killed. Other civilians went missing. The local survivors fled the village towards Komanda locality. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19587)

desc: On 8 December 2020, a group of inhabitants from Bena Tshiloba village, armed with arrows and edged weapons, clashed with armed villagers in the neighboring Bena Kabombu, near Bena Tshiloba (Miabi, Kasai-Oriental). 1 man, the chief of Bena Kabombu, was killed and his body burned, and another man was also killed. 5 other people were seriously injured and 60 houses were burned down. The police were deployed but did not intervene as it was outnumbered by the militiamen. The clash was triggered by longstanding land conflicts. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19577)

desc: On 4 December 2020, FPIC militia rebels armed with firearms and machetes killed at least 20 civilians, including 11 women and children (many of them decapitated), during an attack in Babukela village, near Yombiabo village (Irumu, Ituri). Other locals were reported missing and injured, including at least 3 seriously injured. The assailants were accused of collaborating with the CODECO armed group. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19554)

The survivors fled towards Bule locality. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19525)

desc: On 27 November 2020, a group of students and other people demonstrated in Lusambo locality (Lusambo, Sankuru) against the governor of this province after he had raised the school tuition fees. The police intervened and shot teargas to disperse the students. Some of them confronted the police by throwing stones. 1 person was injured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19475)

desc: On 23 November 2020, a group of local people killed 2 policemen in Yafeta-Yaenge village, within Isangi territory (Isangi, Tshopo). The attack was in reaction to the killing of a man by 1 policeman during a drunken rampage the previous day. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19450)

desc: On 22 November 2020, a group of local people clashed with another group of locals in Bodiawa village, within Gemena territory (Gemena, Sud-Ubangi), following a personal dispute between 2 men over a woman. desc: On 16 November 2020, APCLS armed men clashed with Nyatura (under the CMC alliance) in Kahanga village, near Mutobo (Masisi, Nord-Kivu), over land and tax control. The clash resulted in at least 2 civilians and 7 fighters killed. The local population fled the area. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19389)

actor2: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 13 November 2020, a group of students demonstrated in Gemena town (Gemena, Sud-Ubangui) to show their outrage after a fellow student died in prison
desc: On 13 November 2020, a group of students demonstrated in Gemena town (Gemena, Sud-Ubangui) to show their outrage after a fellow student died in prison due to the lack of medical support. The demonstrators targeted and vandalized the residences of local authorities and other people who they blamed for the incident. The police and the FARDC responded using teargas to disperse the crowd. As they failed, they opened fire against them, resulting in 2 young men killed and 8 injured. Several other demonstrators were arrested. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19338)

desc: On 8 November 2020, Military Forces of Angola killed 7 Angolan refugees (5 men and 2 women) in Yema di Yanga locality, near Tshela (Tshela, Kongo-Central). Reportedly, the army accused the victims, who were unarmed, of being members of the Armed Forces of Cabinda (FLEC-FAC). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19366)

The statement released by the Islamic State said that they had targeted Christians. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19196)

The FARDC drove the rebels out of the area. Overall, at least 27 rebels and 3 FARDC soldiers were killed and 4 soldiers were injured during the 3-day clashes. Several weapons were recovered by the army. Fatalities coded across 3 events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19177)

desc: On 22 October 2020, the FARDC clashed with ADF combatants in Kwele village, near Kainama II & III locality (Beni, Nord-Kivu), following an ambush by the FARDC. 16 people were killed (9 ADF rebels and 7 civilians) and 5 civilian hostages were freed (3 of them were injured during the crossfire). Around 10 rebels were captured, including the wife of one of the leaders of this armed group, Jamil Mukulu. The army also recovered several weapons and other equipment. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19130)

desc: Around 21 October 2020 (week of), suspected ADF militia rebels killed 29 men in Kavuyri village, near Kasese village (Beni, Nord-Kivu). The victims, whose bodies were found on 17 November in an advanced state of decomposition, were said to be among the more than 1,400 men who escaped from Beni prison that month. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19355)

desc: Around 20 October 2020, FPIC combatants clashed with the FARDC and the police in Irumu centre (Irumu, Ituri), following an attack by these rebels. During the clashes, 10 people were killed, 2 PNC officers were killed, as well as 2 FPIC militant, 3 FARDC officers, 2 women and 1 child. 1 woman was also raped by the FPIC. The attackers set on fire the local police station and the public territorial office. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19120)

The attack was the response to previous ethnic-based attacks against Hema people by the FPIC militia, drawn from the Bira ethnic group. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19094)

desc: On 16 October 2020, suspected FPIC militiamen, a militia which claims to defend the interests of the Bira community, attacked the Bakakinda livestock market in Irumu centre (Irumu, Ituri) and killed at least 13 civilians, mostly from the Hema ethnic. The attack is linked to ethnic-based tensions triggered by militia violence, since the FPIC is accused of complicity with CODECO. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19058)

Five of the victims were killed in Mabuho-Mbimbi. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19005)

desc: On 1 October 2020, militiamen from the National Lumumbist Resistance (RNL) clashed with the Mayi-Mayi Congo Ebebi armed men (led by Dragon) in Liboyo village, near Manguredjpa (Lubero, Nord-Kivu), following an attack by the RNL. 5 Mayi-Mayi Congo Ebebi fighters and 1 civilian were killed and several other people were injured, including a woman who was seriously injured. 8 Mayi-Mayi Ebebi were captured by the RNL men (3 of them were executed later on and the rest were minors who were released). The FARDC took control of the village later that day and the locals fled towards Manguredjpa. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18937)

desc: On 1 October 2020, Mayi-Mayi Malaika (led by Sheikh Assan) armed men clashed with the FARDC in Kaselebela village, 10 Km from Saramabila (Kabambare, Maniema), following an ambush by these Mayi-Mayi against a military vehicle on patrol. 2 FARDC soldiers were killed and 5 injured by these rebels. Reportedly, the attack was a response to recent arrests of rebels by the FARDC. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18942)

desc: On 30 September 2020, FRPI (Front for Patriotic Resistance of Ituri) armed men led by Mbadhu Adirodu clashed with the FARDC in the center of Geti village (Irumu, Ituri), following an attack by these rebels against the FARDC. 3 FARDC soldiers, 6 rebels and 2 civilians were killed and 6 rebels and 4 soldiers were seriously injured. The assailants looted and caused important property damage including the building of a local NGO. Reportedly, the attack was in reaction to alleged insults by a FARDC commander against the leader of this militia. This rebel movement signed a peace accord with the government and the UN in February this year. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18925)

desc: On 29 September 2020, a group of policemen set fire to 15 homes belonging to the local population in Inongo town (Inongo, Mai-Ndombe). They did it in reaction to the killing of a fellow policeman, whose death they attributed to locals. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18918)

desc: On 28 September 2020, FARDC soldiers killed 1 man in an area 30 Km from Basoko (Basoko, Tshopo). Reportedly, the man was killed as he refused to pay illegal taxes to the soldiers. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18902)

actor1: Bakata Katanga Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)

title: On 27 September 2020, Bakata Katanga militiamen/prisoners clashed with prison guards for the third day in the Kasapa prison in Lubumbashi (Lubumbashi,
desc: On 27 September 2020, Bakata Katanga militiamen/prisoners clashed with prison guards for the third day in the Kasapa prison in Lubumbashi (Lubumbashi, Haut-Katanga) to liberate prisoners. Reports suggest that the clashes were triggered after 15 inmates pushed other prisoners to violence, burned down several buildings/belongings, and raped over 50 women and at least 6 men/boys. During these clashes, at least 20 people were killed by security forces, including 7 that were executed while trying to escape the prison and 1 prison guard that died from his injuries. Rival inmate groups also clashed against one another over the control of the prison (coded as another event). The police gained control the situation with the help of a group of inmates who detained 40 people (including those that started the riot). 20 fatalities spread across 4 events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19483)

actor1: Mayi Mayi Militia (Bakata Katanga)

desc: On 26 September 2020, around 200 Mayi-Mayi Bakata Katanga militiamen armed with firearms, machetes and arrows clashed with the FARDC and the police during an incursion by these rebels in the center of Lubumbashi city (Lubumbashi, Haut-Katanga). Overall, 16 Mayi-Mayi were killed and many others injured by the state forces, who also captured 13 rebels and repelled the rest of fighters. 2 policemen were killed (decapitated) and 1 FARDC shot and killed. Reportedly, these Katanga pro-independence rebels tried to seize strategic public buildings despite the state's immediate response, whose troops reestablished order a few hours later. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18871)

Rival inmate groups also clashed against one another over the control of the prison (coded as another event). 20 fatalities spread across 4 events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19482)

A brigade from MONUSCO was sent to the scene along with FARDC troops to investigate the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18695)

desc: On 2 September 2020, FPIC armed men clashed with FARDC soldiers in Walu village (Irumu, Ituri), following an incursion by this rebel group. The clash resulted in 8 FPIC fighters and 2 FARDC soldiers killed. The rebels managed to loot several goods from the local residents, who fled towards the forest. The FARDC seized a significant amount of ammunition from the rebels. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18616)

desc: On 2 September 2020, a local communal militia armed with machetes and rifles attacked a group of Fulani people in their camp, 2 km south of Banda locality (Ango, Bas-Uele). The security forces intervened to stop the attack. 4 Fulani people were killed (their bodies were discovered in the bush around the area) and 1 Fulani woman went missing. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18950)

desc: On 31 August 2020, a group of people armed with edged weapons from Lioti-Lokori village clashed with other locals in neighboring Yofeko village (Yahuma, Tshopo). 5 people were killed and other 10 were wounded in the clash. The communal violence was triggered by accusations over the death of a local prominent man by poisoning. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18605)

The rebel offensive was in reaction to the killing of the leader of this group by the FARDC on 27 August. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18596)

desc: On 28 August 2020, the ADF armed with heavy weapons clashed with an FARDC convoy on the main road in Kitaura village, near Matiba (Beni, Nord-Kivu), following an ambush by these rebels. They launched 1 rocket at 2 FARDC vehicles, with 8 soldiers and 2 ADF rebels killed in the ensuing clash. Other sources said that at least 10 rebels were killed. Between 7 and 8 soldiers were injured and many ADF fighters were also injured. During the clash, some ADF men killed at least 4 civilians who were in the fields nearby (3 women and 1 man). The Islamic State Central Africa claimed the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18052)

desc: On 28 August 2020, ADF attacked the FARDC positions of PK3, Kitaura, and Malehe in Mbau town (Beni, Nord-Kivu) over the span of two hours in the evening. During the course of the clash, 7 civilians, 4 FARDC, and 2 ADF were killed, along with two motorcycles and a FARDC jeep burned. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC35901)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces

desc: On 25 August 2020, ADF militiamen killed 28 people during incursions in Maitiki (11), Mabuho (6) and Babila (17) villages, at 12 Km from Mamove locality (Beni, Nord-Kivu). This was one of the various attacks targeting civilians in the area of Mamove on 24 and 25 August. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18560)

actor1: Unidentified Armed Group (Democratic Republic of Congo)

desc: On 21 August 2020, an unidentified armed group abducted and killed 1 man, member of LUCHA, in Kenge (Kenge, Kwango). The victim was one of the organizers of a recent protest against the governor of the province. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18536)

actor1: Mayi Mayi Militia (Hapa na pale)
title: On 19 August 2020, Batwa Mayi-Mayi Hapa Na Pale militiamen shot and killed 11 people and shot and injured 8 others in Kako locality, on the Lulimba-Be
desc: On 19 August 2020, Batwa Mayi-Mayi Hapa Na Pale militiamen shot and killed 11 people and shot and injured 8 others in Kako locality, on the Lulimba-Bendera road, near Lukolo village (Kabambare, Maniema). The attackers, who had set up a barricade on this road, intercepted a vehicle and summarily executed the passengers. The death toll was said to be provisional. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18507)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 19 August 2020, ADF militiamen armed with firearms and machetes killed 13 civilians, including 3 women, during an attack in Matiba village (Beni, N
desc: On 19 August 2020, ADF militiamen armed with firearms and machetes killed 13 civilians, including 3 women, during an attack in Matiba village (Beni, Nord-Kivu). At least 7 residents also went missing. 4 additional lifeless bodies (3 men and 1 woman) were found in the area on 24 August, raising the death toll to 17. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18458)

actor1: Batwa Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)

desc: On 19 August 2020, Batwa ethnic militiamen armed with firearms and arrows killed 2 FARDC soldiers and seriously wounded 2 others during an ambush near Bendera (Kalemie, Tanganyika). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18503)

actor1: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 17 August 2020, 1 policeman shot and killed 1 woman in the Kimbaseke district of Kinshasa (Kinshasa). Reportedly, she was killed following a disput
desc: On 17 August 2020, 1 policeman shot and killed 1 woman in the Kimbaseke district of Kinshasa (Kinshasa). Reportedly, she was killed following a dispute because she did not wear a face mask. Local authorities in this city had just made wearing face-mask mandatory due to the coronavirus pandemic. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18495)

actor1: Mayi Mayi Militia (Yakutumba)
title: On 16 August 2020, Mayi Mayi Yakutumba militiamen clashed with Mayi-Mayi Biloze Bishambuke armed men in the forest of Ekengya Bulembo, near Namisha (F
desc: On 16 August 2020, Mayi Mayi Yakutumba militiamen clashed with Mayi-Mayi Biloze Bishambuke armed men in the forest of Ekengya Bulembo, near Namisha (Fizi, Sud-Kivu), following an offensive by the Yakutumba. The clash resulted in between 8 and 17 fighters killed and several injured from both sides. Many civilians sought refuge into the forest. The clash was caused by cattle rustling by the Biloze Bishambuke. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18486)

actor1: Protesters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 12 August 2020, members and supporters of the FCC political platform, including the PPRD party, marched in Bumba (Bumba, Mongala) to support the na
desc: On 12 August 2020, members and supporters of the FCC political platform, including the PPRD party, marched in Bumba (Bumba, Mongala) to support the national institutions and the constitution, in reaction to widespread demonstrations led by the rival UDPS party the previous month. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18438)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: On 9 August 2020, suspected CODECO combatants killed between 19 and 23 civilians during simultaneous attacks in the neighboring villages of Tchulu, Li
desc: On 9 August 2020, suspected CODECO combatants killed between 19 and 23 civilians during simultaneous attacks in the neighboring villages of Tchulu, Lisey and Aloize, located within Baniari de Kilo chieftaincy, near Kilo village (Djugu, Ituri). 12 people were killed in Aloize, 5 in Linsey and 2 more in Tchulu. Some sources said that 4 other bodies were found in Tchulu later on, raising the death toll to 23. At least 5 civilians were injured and some other locals went missing. The survivors fled these villages towards the forest and safer areas. The FARDC arrived later, when the assailants had already gone. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18413)

actor1: Rioters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 6 August 2020, a group of locals captured and killed a man (burned to death) accused of theft in the city of Mbandaka (Mbandaka, Equateur). The vic
desc: On 6 August 2020, a group of locals captured and killed a man (burned to death) accused of theft in the city of Mbandaka (Mbandaka, Equateur). The victim was part of a group of 3 alleged thieves. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18365)

actor1: Bolempo Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Bena Milombe Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 3 August 2020, a group of armed people of Kuba ethnicity from Bolempo village clashed with armed people from the nearby Bena Milombe village, mostl
desc: On 3 August 2020, a group of armed people of Kuba ethnicity from Bolempo village clashed with armed people from the nearby Bena Milombe village, mostly from Luba ethnic, in Bakuankenge locality, 20 Km from Kakenge (Mweka, Kasai), a disputed boundary area between the provinces of Kasai and Kasai-Central. Between 7 and 11 people were killed, with 17 to 32 injured. More than 300 houses were reportedly destroyed. The local population fled the area. The clashes were triggered by long-standing ethnic and land disputes. The FARDC deployed troops and the MONUSCO sent a fact-finding mission. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18350)

desc: On 1 August 2020, two factions of NDC-R clashed with each other in Kaseke (Walikale, Nord Kivu). 20 were killed and 11 wounded in the clash. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC20981)

desc: On 31 July 2020, NDC-R armed men led by Gilbert Bwira clashed with the NDC-R led by Guidon Shirayi over power and territorial control in Kaseke (Walikale, Nord-Kivu), following an offensive by the Bwira men. About 20 combatants were killed and 11 were wounded in the clash among members of both groups. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18338)

actor1: Mayi Mayi Militia (Hapa na pale)
title: On 30 July 2020, Mayi-Mayi Batwa militiamen (Hapa na pale) killed 2 men and injured 2 others during an incursion in a mining site in Kakulu (Nyunzu, T
desc: On 30 July 2020, Mayi-Mayi Batwa militiamen (Hapa na pale) killed 2 men and injured 2 others during an incursion in a mining site in Kakulu (Nyunzu, Tanganyika). The victims, who had confronted the attackers when they were being robbed, were killed with knives. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18028)

actor1: Kinzau Vuete Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 30 July 2020, a group of local armed men tortured and killed (burned alive) 2 men in Kinzau Vuete (Seke-Banza, Kongo-Central). The victims, twin br
desc: On 30 July 2020, a group of local armed men tortured and killed (burned alive) 2 men in Kinzau Vuete (Seke-Banza, Kongo-Central). The victims, twin brothers from the Luba ethnic group, were reportedly killed because of their ethnicity and affiliation with the UDPS party. 25 men were later prosecuted and 14 of them were found guilty and sentenced to death. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18335)

actor1: Unidentified Armed Group (Democratic Republic of Congo)

desc: On 23 July 2020, an unidentified armed group ambushed a provincial government delegation 15 km west of Banda (Ango, Bas-Uele). The assailants killed 1 government official and his motorcycle driver. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18461)

actor1: NDC-R: Nduma Defence of Congo - Renove (Guidon)
actor2: NDC-R: Nduma Defence of Congo (Bwira Splinter Faction)
title: On 20 July 2020, the NDC-R faction led by Guidon Shimiray clashed with the NDC-R faction of Gilbert Bwira following an offensive by Guidon in Bushimoo
desc: On 20 July 2020, the NDC-R faction led by Guidon Shimiray clashed with the NDC-R faction of Gilbert Bwira following an offensive by Guidon in Bushimoo village, near Pinga (Walikale, Nord-Kivu). At least 2 civilians and more than 15 combatants were killed in the clashes in Katanga, Bushimoo and Nkasa, all near Pinga. 3 days later, at least another 30 civilian and combatant bodies were found. The fighters of Guidon drove the ones of Gilbert Bwira out of the area. More than 700 households sought refuge in Pinga's Hospital. Fatalities split across 2 events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17926)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 16 July 2020, ADF militia rebels armed with machetes and firearms killed 11 civilians during an incursion in Belu, near Bwakadi village (Irumu, Itu
desc: On 16 July 2020, ADF militia rebels armed with machetes and firearms killed 11 civilians during an incursion in Belu, near Bwakadi village (Irumu, Ituri). The assailants looted and set houses on fire. The FARDC were absent in this area. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17887)

actor1: Twirwaneho Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 16 July 2020, the coalition of Banyamulenge militias (Gumino and Twirwaneho) carried out a deadly attack against civilians (all from Babembe, Baful
desc: On 16 July 2020, the coalition of Banyamulenge militias (Gumino and Twirwaneho) carried out a deadly attack against civilians (all from Babembe, Bafuliiru and Banyindu communities) in Kipupu village (Mwenga, Sud-Kivu). There may have been attacks in 4 other nearby villages (not confirmed). 17 women and girls were raped. 15 civilians were killed, including women and children (8 of whom were killed by machetes and 7 who died when their houses were set on fire), 11 were injured and at least 213 people fled or were reported missing. More than 100 houses were burned down and around 80 cows were stolen. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17890)

actor1: NDC-R: Nduma Defence of Congo - Renove (Guidon)
actor2: NDC-R: Nduma Defence of Congo (Bwira Splinter Faction)
title: On 15 July 2020, the NDC-R factions led by Guidon Shimiray on one side and Gilbert Bwira on the other clashed with each other over power and territori
desc: On 15 July 2020, the NDC-R factions led by Guidon Shimiray on one side and Gilbert Bwira on the other clashed with each other over power and territorial control in Kisimba village, near Pinga (Walikale, Nord-Kivu), following an offensive by the Guidon fighters. 5 Guidon men and 12 under Bwira died in the clash, forcing the latter to retreat. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17884)

actor1: FPAC: Ituri Self-Defense Popular Front (Zaire)
title: Around 14 July 2020 (between 30 June - 26 July), armed men from a new formed militia called Zaire, drawn from the Hema ethnic group, carried out vario
desc: Around 14 July 2020 (between 30 June - 26 July), armed men from a new formed militia called Zaire, drawn from the Hema ethnic group, carried out various attacks against civilians from Bira ethnic in various unspecified villages within Irumu territory (Ituri). Reportedly, around 30 civilians were killed and around 20 villages were attacked and some of its houses burned down. These ethnic-based attacks were triggered by violent reprisals linked to militias and disputes over land and natural resources. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17993)

actor1: Chini Ya Kilima-FPIC: Patriotic and Integrationist Force of Congo

desc: Around 14 July 2020 (between 30 June - 26 July), FPIC militiamen, drawn from the Bira ethnic group, carried out various attacks against civilians from Hema ethnic in the villages of Susenga, Djakada, Murubia and Kabarole, all within Irumu territory (Ituri). Reportedly, more than 20 people were killed and around 200 cows were looted. These ethnic-based attacks were triggered by violent reprisals linked to militias and disputes over land and natural resources. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17994)

actor1: NDC-R: Nduma Defence of Congo (Bwira Splinter Faction)
actor2: NDC-R: Nduma Defence of Congo - Renove (Guidon)

desc: On 13 July 2020, clashes between 2 NDC-R factions, led by Guidon Shimiray and Gilbert Bwira, took place in Mutongo (Walikale, Nord-Kivu), following the demotion of the commander Guidon Shimiray by his fellow deputies (led by Gilbert Bwira). 12 rebels were killed and 3 were wounded during the crossfire. The local population fled the area. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17872)

actor1: Rioters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 9 July 2020, thousands of members of the ruling UDPS, Lucha, Filimbi and other associations marched near parliament in Kinshasa against the endorse
desc: On 9 July 2020, thousands of members of the ruling UDPS, Lucha, Filimbi and other associations marched near parliament in Kinshasa against the endorsement of Ronsard Malonda as chairperson of the National Independent Electoral Commission. Some demonstrators erected barricades with burning tires and confronted the police with stones and petrol bombs. 2 demonstrators and 1 policeman were killed in clashes, with at least 27 people injured, including 19 policemen. Public and private property and political party offices were destroyed. Several arrests were made, including 1 journalist. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17793)

desc: On 9 July 2020, tens of supporters of the UDPS party marched in Kamina (Haut-Lomami) against the endorsement of Ronsard Malonda as chairperson of the CENI (National Independent Electoral Commission). No incidents were reported. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17787)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo

desc: On 8 July 2020, between 25 and 37 people were killed and many others seriously wounded in an attack by CODECO militia combatants in Bunzenzele village (Djugu, Ituri). Between 400 and 500 people, including women and children, reportedly participated in the attack. At least 15 houses were burned. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17770)

actor1: Rioters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 7 July 2020, two groups of local residents clashed in Masamuna (Masi-Manimba, Kwilu) over a debt payment dispute, leaving 1 man dead. The police in
desc: On 7 July 2020, two groups of local residents clashed in Masamuna (Masi-Manimba, Kwilu) over a debt payment dispute, leaving 1 man dead. The police intervened but they could not prevent some men from vandalizing their post and set on fire the residence of the police commander. 1 police officer resulted injured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17761)

desc: On 5 July 2020, ADF militiamen armed with firearms and machetes killed 13 civilians during an incursion in Kainama village (Beni, Nord-Kivu). They also abducted 41 villagers and torched houses. The 41 abducted people were released 2 weeks later. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18522)

desc: On 5 July 2020, APCLS and Nyatura APRDC fighters attacked the NDC-R in Buboa (Masisi, Nord-Kivu). The APCLS-Nyatura APRDC coalition drove the NDC-R from the village. The attack killed six NDC-R militiamen, four APCLS fighters, and two civilians. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC43909)

desc: On 5 July 2020, the FARDC clashed with Angolan border police forces near the border in Kamonia territory (Kamonia, Kasai), resulting in 1 Angolan agent killed and 1 FARDC soldier injured. The clash occurred after a group of Angolan soldiers crossed into DRC territory. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17742)

desc: On 4 July 2020, CODECO clashed with military and police forces in Matete, between Gina and Pitso (Djugu, Ituri). 2 soldiers, 3 police officers and 6 civilians were killed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17700)

desc: On 2 July 2020, Mai Mai Hapa Na Pale militiamen, a Batwa ethnic militia, killed 12 civilians (3 women and 9 men) and injured 6 others by arrows in Mugunga village, near Lengwe (Nyunzu, Tanganyika). This attack is linked to the long-lasting inter-ethnic conflict between the Bantu and the Batwa ethnic groups in Tanganyika area. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17689)

desc: On 2 July 2020, FARDC elements clashed with a group of Batwa Mayi Mayi militiamen (Hapa na pale) near Mugunga, a few Km from Lengwe (Nyunzu, Tanganyika), where these rebels had carried out a deadly attack against civilians. The army shot and killed 6 rebels. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17730)

desc: On 29 June 2020, CODECO militiamen attacked civilians in Mugulumoya and Kibangu villages in Babelebe chiefdom (Irumu, Ituri), armed with firearms and machetes. 24 civilians (including women and children) were killed, 3 injured and 2 kidnapped. desc: On 19 June 2020, during a two day battle, the Front for the Liberation of the Enclave of Cabinda-Armed Forces of Cabinda (FLEC-FAC) clashed with Angolan military forces in Mbata-Mbenge (Lukula, Kongo-Central). 11 people were killed, of whom 7 were civilians and 4 soldiers. 11 fatalities split over 2 days. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17636)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
actor2: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo

desc: On 16 June 2020, the FARDC clashed with CODECO militiamen during a military offensive at Riti-Wari village in Irumu (Irumu, Ituri), resulting in 15 rebels killed and many others wounded, as well as 3 soldiers injured. Five AK-47 rifles were also seized by the army. The militia was finally repelled by the army. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17536)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 14 June 2020, presumed ADF rebels raided Mukondi village 20km east of Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu). A dozen people were reportedly killed and houses
desc: On 14 June 2020, presumed ADF rebels raided Mukondi village 20km east of Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu). A dozen people were reportedly killed and houses were burned. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC18308)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 12 June 2020, the FARDC clashed with ADF combatants during an offensive launched by the army in Bambuba-Kisiki village (Beni, Nord-Kivu), resulting
desc: On 12 June 2020, the FARDC clashed with ADF combatants during an offensive launched by the army in Bambuba-Kisiki village (Beni, Nord-Kivu), resulting in several rebels shot and killed. The FARDC congratulated the collaboration of the local population for the success of the operation. Several fatalities coded as 3. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17476)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: On 10 June 2020, CODECO militiamen armed with machetes and firearms killed 9 civilians, including 3 men, 1 woman and 5 children, during an attack in L
desc: On 10 June 2020, CODECO militiamen armed with machetes and firearms killed 9 civilians, including 3 men, 1 woman and 5 children, during an attack in Lenga village (Djugu, Ituri) when the victims were sleeping. At least 150 houses were burned down and some residents fled after the dead were buried. The FARDC was not deployed in this village. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17466)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
actor2: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: Around 5 June 2020 (as reported), the FARDC regained control of 23 villages of the Walendu-Watsi chiefdom (Mahagi, Ituri) after clashes with the CODEC
desc: Around 5 June 2020 (as reported), the FARDC regained control of 23 villages of the Walendu-Watsi chiefdom (Mahagi, Ituri) after clashes with the CODECO militia. Many militiamen were reportedly killed. Many fatalities coded as 10. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17326)

actor1: NDC-R: Nduma Defence of Congo - Renove (Guidon)
actor2: FDLR: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda

desc: On 3 June 2020, the NDC-R and the FDLR clashed in Mashango village (Walikale, Nord-Kivu), close to the border with Rutshuru territory. The crossfire ended up with 27 fighters killed and many more injured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17379)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: On 3 June 2020, CODECO militiamen armed with weapons and machetes killed 16 civilians, including 7 women, 5 girls (all aged below 5) and 4 men, during
desc: On 3 June 2020, CODECO militiamen armed with weapons and machetes killed 16 civilians, including 7 women, 5 girls (all aged below 5) and 4 men, during an attack in Moussa village, which is home mainly to ethnic Hema, within Mambisa chiefdom (Djugu, Ituri). The assailants also looted 7 goats and other goods. The local population fled towards neighboring villages. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17377)

actor1: Kilembwe Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Kapowa Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: Around 3 June 2020 (week of), a group of local people from Kilembwe village clashed with others from the neighboring Kapowa village, both in Lubao chi
desc: Around 3 June 2020 (week of), a group of local people from Kilembwe village clashed with others from the neighboring Kapowa village, both in Lubao chiefdom (Lubao, Lomami), over a territorial dispute to access a fishing area of a river. During one clash, a man from Kilembwe was killed (beheaded with a machete) and then as a response the relatives of the victim attempted to torch houses in Kapowa. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17440)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
actor2: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo

desc: On 2 June 2020, The FARDC clashed with the CODECO armed group in Basani village, near Linga (Ituri), resulting in 18 rebels and 1 soldier killed. Another soldier was severely injured. The army also recovered 7 weapons and several munitions. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17353)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 2 June 2020, ADF armed men reportedly killed 11 people who were part of a gathering of Christians in a village in the Rwenzori area (Beni, Nord-Kiv
desc: On 2 June 2020, ADF armed men reportedly killed 11 people who were part of a gathering of Christians in a village in the Rwenzori area (Beni, Nord-Kivu). The Islamic State Central Africa claimed the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17354)

actor1: Mayi Mayi Militia (Biloze Bishambuke)
actor2: Banyamulenge Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 31 May 2020, the Mai-Mai Biloze Bishambuke clashed with an unidentified Banyamulenge Ethnic Militia in the villages of Kangwa, Cakira,Karunga, Kaba
desc: On 31 May 2020, the Mai-Mai Biloze Bishambuke clashed with an unidentified Banyamulenge Ethnic Militia in the villages of Kangwa, Cakira,Karunga, Kabara and Kamombo (Fizi, Sud-Kivu). Thirteen Banyamulenge fighters were killed and 2 captured. On the Mayi-Mayi side, 2 were killed and 1 wounded. Five of the fatalities were reported in Cakira village (coded separately). Two civilian women were also wounded. The clash was prompted by ethnic and land-use tensions during the transhumance period. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17395)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 28 May 2020, FARDC Forces clashed with ADF militiamen near Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu) resulting in 10 rebel and 3 military deaths. Three other sold
desc: On 28 May 2020, FARDC Forces clashed with ADF militiamen near Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu) resulting in 10 rebel and 3 military deaths. Three other soldiers were seriously injured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17264)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 26 May 2020, ADF reportedly killed at least 40 civilians during simultaneous attacks in Samboko and Tikamaibo villages, both in Walese Vonkutu chie
desc: On 26 May 2020, ADF reportedly killed at least 40 civilians during simultaneous attacks in Samboko and Tikamaibo villages, both in Walese Vonkutu chiefdom (Irumu, Ituri). The dead bodies were found hours later by locals. The assailants also looted food and other valuables. Casualties split between the two attacks. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17254)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 26 May 2020, ADF armed men clashed with the FARDC in Otokaka-Makembi village, 1 Km from Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu), resulting in 23 FARDC soldiers
desc: On 26 May 2020, ADF armed men clashed with the FARDC in Otokaka-Makembi village, 1 Km from Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu), resulting in 23 FARDC soldiers and 7 rebels killed. The Islamic State Central Africa claimed the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17246)

actor1: Gumino Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: Around 25 May 2020 (from 23 - 26 May), a coalition of Banyamulenge armed militias (Gumino and Twirwaneho) clashed with the FARDC in Kakenge and Ilungu
desc: Around 25 May 2020 (from 23 - 26 May), a coalition of Banyamulenge armed militias (Gumino and Twirwaneho) clashed with the FARDC in Kakenge and Ilungu villages as well as other villages near Minembwe (Mwenga, Sud-Kivu). The clashes resulted in 21 FARDC, 5 militiamen and 2 civilian fatalities. Three soldiers, 7 rebels and 1 civilian woman were wounded. The clashes reportedly took place after a soldier accused of looting crops belonging to Banyamulenge was shot by Twirwaneho men. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17237)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 25 May 2020, ADF militiamen clashed with FARDC elements during an incursion in Loselose village, 10 Km from Mutwanga (Beni, Nord-Kivu) resulting in
desc: On 25 May 2020, ADF militiamen clashed with FARDC elements during an incursion in Loselose village, 10 Km from Mutwanga (Beni, Nord-Kivu) resulting in the deaths of 7 civilians, including 2 women, and 2 soldiers. The assailants also looted and set fire to 6 houses. Reportedly, tens of other locals went missing. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17238)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 24 May 2020, ADF militiamen armed with weapons and machetes killed between 10 and 17 civilians, including at least 5 women, during an attack in Kec
desc: On 24 May 2020, ADF militiamen armed with weapons and machetes killed between 10 and 17 civilians, including at least 5 women, during an attack in Kechele and Makutano villages, both 10 Km from Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu). The assailants looted several goods from the locals and then torched the houses. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17224)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
actor2: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo

desc: On 23 May 2020, FARDC forces shot and killed 13 CODECO militiamen during a clash on the road near Linga (Djugu, Ituri). One soldier was also killed and 6 others injured. The army recovered weapons and machetes from the victims. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17222)

actor1: Bena Mulamba Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Bena Lwanga Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 21 May 2020, local civilians from Bena Mulamba village fought with locals from Bena Lwanga, in Ngandajika territory (Lomami), over a land-use confl
desc: On 21 May 2020, local civilians from Bena Mulamba village fought with locals from Bena Lwanga, in Ngandajika territory (Lomami), over a land-use conflict. During the confrontations 2 men from Bena Mulamba were killed and another was seriously injured, triggering a reprisal attack that ended up with 68 houses of Bena Lwanga burnt down, forcing its residents to flee the village. The police were deployed and controlled the situation. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17312)

actor1: CMC: Coalition of Movements of Change of the Congo
actor2: NDC-R: Nduma Defence of Congo - Renove (Guidon)

desc: On 18 May 2020, APCLS, Nyatura APRDC, Nyatura Domi, and FDLR-FOCA fighters, all under the CMC coalition, clashed with the NDC-R at Bwenderu village (Walikale, Nord-Kivu) following an offensive by the CMC coalition. The clash left 8 NDC-R fighters and 5 CMC fighters dead. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17497)

actor1: Mamba Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Kabwe Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: Around 18 May 2020 (week of), locals from Kabwe village fought with locals from Mamba village, both in Baluba locality, Lusambo territory (Sankuru), o
desc: Around 18 May 2020 (week of), locals from Kabwe village fought with locals from Mamba village, both in Baluba locality, Lusambo territory (Sankuru), over a customary power conflict. During the confrontations 1 woman from Mamba was killed, triggering a reprisal attack that ended up with all the houses of Kabwe burnt down, forcing all its residents to flee the village. Police officers were deployed to calm down the situation. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17300)

actor1: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 18 May 2020, a group of alleged bandits shot and killed 2 civilians during a robbery in 2 properties in the Mutoshi district of Kolwezi (Kolwezi, L
desc: On 18 May 2020, a group of alleged bandits shot and killed 2 civilians during a robbery in 2 properties in the Mutoshi district of Kolwezi (Kolwezi, Lualaba). The victims were part of a group of local unarmed Vigilantes who had confronted the robbers. Most of the perpetrators were later arrested by the state authorities, who admitted that the bandits were Police and FARDC elements. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17298)

actor1: Unidentified Armed Group (Democratic Republic of Congo)

desc: On 17 May 2020, an unidentified group attacked Jisa/Wad'abu (Djugu, Ituri), killing 20 ethnically-Hema people. A further 17 were injured and 2 abducted. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17632)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: On 16 May 2020, CODECO militiamen armed with guns and machetes killed 21 civilians, including 4 women and 6 children, and injured 5 more during an att
desc: On 16 May 2020, CODECO militiamen armed with guns and machetes killed 21 civilians, including 4 women and 6 children, and injured 5 more during an attack in the village of Wadabu (predominantly Hema ethnic community), 4 Km from Largu village (Djugu, Ituri). (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC59)

actor1: LRA: Lords Resistance Army
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 13 May 2020, LRA armed men clashed with the FARDC 10 km east of Mabia (Bondo, Bas-Uele) during an ambush by these rebels in which 1 taxi driver was
desc: On 13 May 2020, LRA armed men clashed with the FARDC 10 km east of Mabia (Bondo, Bas-Uele) during an ambush by these rebels in which 1 taxi driver was killed. The soldier was shot and injured but he managed to kill 1 rebel and drive off the assailants. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17281)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 12 May 2020, ADF rebels killed 4 people including 3 women and 1 man in an attack in Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu). 7 civilians, 5 of them Batwa, were
desc: On 12 May 2020, ADF rebels killed 4 people including 3 women and 1 man in an attack in Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu). 7 civilians, 5 of them Batwa, were abducted and then killed by the assailants, as their bodies were found by the FARDC on 14 May near Eringeti. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC43)

actor1: Rioters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Mbororo Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 9 May 2020, a group of local civilians clashed with Mbororo militiamen in Kongo village, 30 Km from Niangara (Haut-Uele, Dungu), resulting in 2 loc
desc: On 9 May 2020, a group of local civilians clashed with Mbororo militiamen in Kongo village, 30 Km from Niangara (Haut-Uele, Dungu), resulting in 2 local people and 1 Mbororo man killed. The incident is believed to be caused by cohabitation conflicts, as the Mbororo are often accused of theft. 6 of them were arrested by the police 3 days later, and then the provincial government decided to expel the Mbororo from this area. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC19)

actor1: Rioters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 8 May 2020, a group of artisanal palm oil producers and traders (some of them with machetes) confronted the police in Binga (Lisala, Mongala) after
desc: On 8 May 2020, a group of artisanal palm oil producers and traders (some of them with machetes) confronted the police in Binga (Lisala, Mongala) after the latter had seized a mill from them to restrict artisanal production. The police responded with aerial firing to disperse them and shot and killed a demonstrator. As a response, the crowd torched the police station and 1 prisoner was killed by the flames. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17192)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
actor2: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo

desc: On 7 May 2020, the FARDC took control of a CODECO militia stronghold in the locality of Bese, near Saliboko (Djugu, Ituri), after clashes with these militiamen which resulted in 23 rebels killed, 12 captured and 7 AK47 weapons recovered by the army. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17189)

actor1: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-) Eco-Guards

desc: On 7 May 2020, eco-guards of the Upemba National Park killed a 16-year-old child in the park, near Lake Upemba (Bukama, Haut-Lomami). The motive behind the attack was not reported. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC11)

actor1: Chini Ya Kilima-FPIC: Patriotic and Integrationist Force of Congo
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: Around 4 May 2020, the Patriotic and Integrationist Force of Congo (FPIC) clashed with FARDC at a military camp in Marabo (Irumu, Ituri) resulting in
desc: Around 4 May 2020, the Patriotic and Integrationist Force of Congo (FPIC) clashed with FARDC at a military camp in Marabo (Irumu, Ituri) resulting in 10 fatalities, including 4 rebels, 4 soldiers and 2 civilians (2 children). At least 2 FARDC and 6 rebels were injured, and 2 other rebels captured. Some sources stated that between 8-12 rebels were killed in addition to the other fatalities. The FPIC cadres looted several weapons and set on fire some huts during the clash. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17169)

actor1: Unidentified Armed Group (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 3 May 2020, an unknown number of armed men, in both military and civilian clothes, raped a woman, killed at least 6 civilians and injured 4 more du
desc: On 3 May 2020, an unknown number of armed men, in both military and civilian clothes, raped a woman, killed at least 6 civilians and injured 4 more during a robbery of at least 30 houses in Lubumbashi 's Annex district (Lubumbashi, Haut-Katanga). The child of the raped woman was abducted by the assailants. There could be more victims as investigations are ongoing. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17164)

actor1: LRA: Lords Resistance Army
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 30 April 2020, an LRA group comprised of men and women clashed with 2 FARDC elements during an ambush carried out by the rebels against 35 people n
desc: On 30 April 2020, an LRA group comprised of men and women clashed with 2 FARDC elements during an ambush carried out by the rebels against 35 people near Soki, 25 Km north of Niangara (Dungu, Haut-Uele). The 2 soldiers were killed and the rest of the civilians were robbed by the assailants. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17275)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 28 April 2020, FARDC soldiers recovered territory following a clash with the ADF at Kambi ya Yua (Beni, Nord-Kivu) resulting in 25 ADF fighters and
desc: On 28 April 2020, FARDC soldiers recovered territory following a clash with the ADF at Kambi ya Yua (Beni, Nord-Kivu) resulting in 25 ADF fighters and 6 military fatalities. The military recovered hand-crafted bombs and assault rifles abandoned by the ADF. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17156)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 28 April 2020, CODECO militiamen clashed with FARDC soldiers and MONUSCO peacekeepers in Saliboko (Djugu, Ituri), resulting in 9 rebels killed and
desc: On 28 April 2020, CODECO militiamen clashed with FARDC soldiers and MONUSCO peacekeepers in Saliboko (Djugu, Ituri), resulting in 9 rebels killed and 3 FARDC soldiers injured. The clashes started after the rebels attacked a MONUSCO convoy that was supporting a FARDC position in the village. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17131)

actor1: LRA: Lords Resistance Army
title: On 27 April 2020, LRA armed men shot and killed 3 civilians in Kapili (Dungu, Haut-Uele) after the rebels had looted some households in the village an
desc: On 27 April 2020, LRA armed men shot and killed 3 civilians in Kapili (Dungu, Haut-Uele) after the rebels had looted some households in the village and some locals had confronted them. There is no FARDC presence in this area. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17126)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
actor2: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: On 26 April 2020, FARDC soldiers clashed with CODECO militiamen in Kabakaba, Esasi and Lisey villages, all within Djugu territory (Djugu, Ituri), resu
desc: On 26 April 2020, FARDC soldiers clashed with CODECO militiamen in Kabakaba, Esasi and Lisey villages, all within Djugu territory (Djugu, Ituri), resulting in 12 rebel, 2 military and 2 civilian fatalities. FARDC managed to drive the rest of the rebels off. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17117)

actor1: Mayi Mayi Militia (Yakutumba)
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)

desc: Between 24 and 25 April 2020, Mayi Mayi Yakutumba militiamen clashed with FARDC forces near Minembwe (Mwenga, Sud-Kivu) leaving a toll of 4 soldiers and 8 rebels dead as well as 11 soldiers injured. At least 2 Mayi Mayi were captured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17091)

actor1: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo
title: On 24 April 2020, CODECO militiamen armed with guns and machetes attacked the village of Ngurahi, near Berunda (Mahagi, Ituri) and killed at least 21
desc: On 24 April 2020, CODECO militiamen armed with guns and machetes attacked the village of Ngurahi, near Berunda (Mahagi, Ituri) and killed at least 21 civilians and injured 6. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17084)

actor1: Unidentified Armed Group (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-) Eco-Guards
title: On 24 April 2020, around 60 unidentified armed men killed 13 ICCN park rangers (Virunga National Park) and 5 civilians in an ambush on the road near M
desc: On 24 April 2020, around 60 unidentified armed men killed 13 ICCN park rangers (Virunga National Park) and 5 civilians in an ambush on the road near Mahura (Rutshuru, Nord-Kivu), few Km from Rumangabo, the Park HQ. The rangers had reportedly gone out to rescue a civilian convoy when they found themselves in the middle of an ambush. The assailants are suspected FDLR cadres. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17088)

actor1: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 24 April 2020, police forces clashed with BDK in the Ngaliema commune of Kinshasa after hours of failed negotiations. Police carried out a special
desc: On 24 April 2020, police forces clashed with BDK in the Ngaliema commune of Kinshasa after hours of failed negotiations. Police carried out a special operation to arrest the leader of the Bundu dia Kongo in his residence. This left 8 dead, at least 35 injured and 168 detained. 8 policemen were also severely injured. The leader was finally arrested alive. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17089)

actor1: BDK: Bundu dia Kongo
actor2: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)

desc: On 22 April 2020, members of Bundu Dia Kongo clashed with the police in Sangololo (Sangololo, Kongo-Central) resulting in 14 BDK members killed, 2 others injured and 7 policemen seriously injured. Some sources claim that BDK was planning to attack Songololo's Territory administrator. The police found several guns, ammunition and arrows tipped with poison and arrested 3 BDK members. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17079)

actor1: FDLR: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda
title: On 19 April 2020, a battalion of FDLR combatants clashed with the FARDC and Rwandan military forces, in an attempt to retake Kazoroho village, in Tong
desc: On 19 April 2020, a battalion of FDLR combatants clashed with the FARDC and Rwandan military forces, in an attempt to retake Kazoroho village, in Tongo groupement (Rutshuru, Nord-Kivu). The clash left 18 FDLR combatants killed and 5 others injured. The latter were taken to the hospital in Kiwanja by MONUSCO military forces. One FDLR high commander was reportedly captured. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17058)

actor1: FDLR: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
title: On 18 April 2020, FDLR rebels attacked Congolese and Rwandan military positions at the village of Kazaroho (3 Km afar from Tongo locality, Rusthsuru,
desc: On 18 April 2020, FDLR rebels attacked Congolese and Rwandan military positions at the village of Kazaroho (3 Km afar from Tongo locality, Rusthsuru, Nord-Kivu), resulting in at least 15 soldiers killed. 3 vehicles from the Rwandan military were destroyed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17052)

actor1: FDLR: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)

desc: On 18 April 2020, the FDLR-FOCA ambushed an FARDC and RDF convoy near Kasali (Rutshuru, Nord-Kivu). Two FDLR-FOCA, four RDF and nine FARDC soldiers were killed in the ambush. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC45482)

actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-) Special Forces
actor2: FDLR: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda

desc: On 15 April 2020, FARDC clashed with FDLR in one of their strongholds in Kilinga (Gihondo Groupement), Rutshuru territory, killing 43 combatants including one high commander. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16999)

At least 4 civilians were injured and 4 more went missing. The assailants also looted several houses. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16984)

desc: On 10 April 2020, FARDC forces clashed with CODECO militiamen across the villages of Dyaro, Lipri and Tcheibi, near Djugu (Ituri), after the latter attempted to carry out incursions on the said locations. The provisional death toll was 33 fatalities across the three villages. Others were injured, and several weapons were seized by army officials. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16970)

desc: On 10 April 2020, FARDC forces clashed with CODECO militiamen in Dala, near Bunia (Ituri), after the latter attempted to carry out incursions on the village. Officials repelled the attack after the militiamen slit the throats of 17 people and killed two FARDC soldiers. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16969)

desc: On 9 April 2020, a group of people carried out popular justice by killing a police officer in the village of Mpanda, near Kabeya Kamwanga, after the officer was caught stealing food. Other policemen retaliated against the village inhabitants burning 5 houses and forcing them to flee for 2 days. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16976)

desc: On 8 April 2020, FARDC forces clashed with CODECO militiamen simultaneously in the villages of Dyaro and Lonjango, near Budu (Ituri). It was reported army officials carried out the attacks to drive the rebels out of the locations to regain these two strongholds, eventually recapturing both. The provisional death toll resulted in 14 fatalities, including 11 CODECO elements, a militia's priest and two FARDC officials. Army officials also arrested 3 militiamen and seized 4 AK-47 weapons and motorbikes. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16959)

desc: On 3 April 2020, Nyatura-Domi (under the CMC alliance) attacked an NDC-R position in Kamudoka village (Rutshuru territory). The Nyatura Domi drove the NDC-R out of the village. 12 NDC-R fighters were killed in the attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC17016)

desc: On 30 March 2020, dozens of members of the religious movement Bundu dia Kongo (BDK) clashed with police forces in Kinshasa (Kinshasa), during an unauthorized march due to coronavirus. It was reported followers were marching to perform mystical rites. Tension started to mount and followers threw stones to police officials, who used teargas and shot in the air to disperse participants. Between 4 and 5 followers were killed and several injured. BDK followers denied violence on their part, and police officials discounted the death toll. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16865)

desc: Between 27 and 28 March 2020, Bakata Katanga militiamen clashed with Police and FARDC officials in Kasumbalesa (Haut-Katanga). Previous to this armed confrontation, militiamen had also clashed with armed forces in Lubumbashi, Likasi and Kakanda.The provisional death toll across Kasumbalesa, Lubumbashi and Likasi resulted in 30 fatalities and several injured. The number of fatalities is spread over the three events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16825)

desc: Between 27 and 28 March 2020, Bakata Katanga militiamen clashed with Police and FARDC officials in Likasi (Haut-Katanga), after an attack carried out by the former against a detention center. Previous to this armed confrontation, militiamen had also clashed with armed forces in Lubumbashi, Kasumbalesa and Kakanda. The provisional death toll across Lubumbashi, Kasumbalesa and Likasi resulted in 30 fatalities and several injured. The number of fatalities is spread over the three events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16826)

actor1: Bakata Katanga Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)

desc: Between 27 and 28 March 2020, Bakata Katanga militiamen clashed with Police and FARDC officials in Lubumbashi (Haut-Katanga), following an incursion perpetrated by the former to attempt a coup. Militiamen continued their way to Likasi, Kasumbalesa and Kakanda.The provisional death toll across Lubumbashi, Kasumbalesa and Likasi resulted in 30 fatalities and several injured. The number of fatalities is spread over the three events. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16827)

desc: Between 27 and 28 March 2020, Bakata Katanga militiamen clashed with Police and FARDC officials in Bunkeya, near Kalundu (Lualaba), after the former raised the Katanga flag at the chiefdom's office. Same incursions took place simultaneously in Lubumbashi (Haut-Katanga) Likasi (Haut-Katanga), Kasumbalesa (Haut-Katanga) and Kakanda (Lualaba). Two militiamen were killed, and another three managed to escape. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16824)

desc: On 27 March 2020, CODECO elements and FARDC forces clashed in Banana and the Nouveau trading center, near Mambisa (Ituri), following an incursion perpetrated by the former. The provisional death toll included 17 fatalities. Several civilians were also injured. It was reported the incursion took place as a reprisal for the killing of the CODECO militia chief last March 25. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16820)

desc: Around 21 March 2020 (between 19 March - 23 March), FARDC forces clashed with ADF fighters near the localities of Kamango, Eringeti and Mbau (Nord-Kivu). Following the clashes, Army forces conquered World Space, a former stronghold of ADF allies located in the area. 49 were killed in the clash, including 37 ADF fighters and 12 FARDC soldiers. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16805)

desc: Between 15 and 16 March 2020, CODECO militiamen carried out attacks in the villages of Dego, Banana and Tshuba near Djugu (Ituri), killing 17 civilians and leaving some others missing across the said communities. Perpetrators also burned houses and looted different establishments. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16749)

actor2: CODECO: Cooperative for Development of Congo

desc: On 14 March 2020, an armed clash took place between FARDC forces and CODECO fighters following a CODECO attack in Tchele, 80 km away from Bunia (Irumu, Ituri). During the clashes, 12 CODECO fighters and a FARDC official were killed. Subsequent reports suggest that, during the attack, the CODECO raped and badly injured a 17-year-old Hema girl. The combatants also raped her 4-year-old girl before killing her with a machete. The CODECO killed a young Hema boy, along with his parents, four siblings and cousins, all of whom were under 14 years of age. Overall, at least 21 people were killed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16732)

desc: On 13 March 2020, a heavy shoot-out took place between FARDC forces and CODECO militiamen in downtown Beni (Nord-Kivu). This took place in the context of ongoing military operations to destroy rebel positions in the east of the city and along the Semuliki river. It was reported 19 CODECO fighters and 8 soldiers were killed, although the dead toll was expected to rise as armed clashes were ongoing. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16728)

desc: Between 6 and 7 March 2020, Military forces and ADF rebels engaged in an armed clash along the Semuliki river, east of the city of Beni (North Kivu). 17 fatalities were reported, including 13 rebels and 4 Congolese soldiers. Heavy detonations and gunshots were reportedly heard during Saturday 7 in the forests of Mayangose. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16666)

A total of six civilians were killed and four injured, with some other kidnapped as well across these two villages on the same day. 5 other bodies were found in the area on 26 July 2020, all members of the same family believed to have been killed by ADF in the same attack. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC16648)

The clash occurred as a local administrator, accompanied by soldiers, went to the home of a Kamwina Nsapu leader to secure the release of an abducted Tetela community leader. The militia reportedly resisted and surrounded the administrator, prompting the FARDC to open fire. Fatalities reported by the hospital reached 19. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13714)

Four militia members were killed, and some sources report one military fatality (spread across the two events). Local civil society organizations indicated that the FARDC does not distinguish between civilians and militia members and has utilized the populations of Babuma and Bayelo villages to loot Lowa. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13716)

Other sources indicated as many as six fatalities. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13351)

desc: 19 December. Rodrigue Ndakazieka, a journalist from Radio Mwinda (owned by a member of the Lamuka Coalition) was detained by police who provide security to Antoinette Ipulu, the National Deputy of the FCC. The police brought the journalist to the politician's house, where they beat him for several hours. He was later taken to a police station in Kinshasa. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13273)

actor1: Batende Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: 17 December. Following the attack on Yumbi city on 16/12, suspected Batende militia members attacked Bongende, Yumbi. The assailants mutilated the vic
desc: 17 December. Following the attack on Yumbi city on 16/12, suspected Batende militia members attacked Bongende, Yumbi. The assailants mutilated the victims, removing limbs and sexual organs of men, women and children. Fetuses were cut out of pregnant women. At least one woman was raped and two children were reportedly abducted. A MONUSCO investigation confirmed 348 fatalities in Bongende, however, they suspected the actual number to be higher. Investigations found that the attacked on the Banunu were likely planned in advance and soldiers participated in the attacks on Bongende. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13360)

actor1: Batende Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2001-2019) Naval Forces
title: 17 December. Suspected Batende militia members attacked Nkolo 2, a Banunu community. Two naval officers attempted to repel the attackers, but one of t
desc: 17 December. Suspected Batende militia members attacked Nkolo 2, a Banunu community. Two naval officers attempted to repel the attackers, but one of the soldiers was killed and the other is missing. Much of the Nkolo 2 population had fled following the Yumbi city attack on 16/12. A MONUSCO investigation confirmed at least 10 fatalities in Nkolo 2. Homes, churches and schools were destroyed. Investigations found that the attacked on the Banunu were likely planned in advance. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13269)

actor1: Batende Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: 17 December. Following the communal attacks in Yumbi, suspected Batende militia members attacked Banunu members in Camp Nbanzi (no coordinates availab
desc: 17 December. Following the communal attacks in Yumbi, suspected Batende militia members attacked Banunu members in Camp Nbanzi (no coordinates available; 70 km from Yumbi). At least seven people were killed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13899)

actor1: Batende Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Banunu Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: 16 December. A group of 3,000 armed Batende attacked Yumbi city - particularly the neighborhoods of Noyi, Bolu, Likolo, and Ntsentseke. Local police m
desc: 16 December. A group of 3,000 armed Batende attacked Yumbi city - particularly the neighborhoods of Noyi, Bolu, Likolo, and Ntsentseke. Local police members participated along the Batende (the head of the police had disarmed Banunu officers prior to the attack). Armed Banunu members retliated in some areas with support from a member of the naval forces. Civilians were shot, stabbed, mutilated, and burned to death; over 900 properties were damaged including homes, schools and churches. A MONUSCO investigation confirmed 170 fatalities, primarily from the Banunu community (the number may be higher due to uncounted bodies dumped into the river). The attack came after a communal dispute over the burial of the Banunu's customary chief on land belonging to Batende on 14/12. Local CSOs indicate that the group's political allegiances of the Batende with the FCC and Banunu with the Lamuka Coalition exacerbated the dispute. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13359)

actor1: Batende Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)

desc: On 16 of December 2018, in Mansele (coordinates are approximate), members of the Batende community killed one Banunu man from Bongende. This prompted four of the seven elements of the FARDC naval forces based in Yumbi to travel to Bongende to investigate. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC14855)

desc: 16 December. UDPS supporters gathered at the airport in Tshikapa for the arrival of presidential candidate Tshisekedi, when FCC supporters began throwing objects at the opposition members. The groups clashed, one person was killed by stones and another, believed to be a UDPS supporter, was beaten to death. Several others were injured. Police utilized tear gas to restore order. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13250)

The bodies were recovered and brought back to Kalemie on November 17th, 2018. The means of attack consisted of bodily assault by stabbing and shooting. It is currently unknown who the perpetrators were and what the motive was for the attack.' (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC14781)

desc: On 14 November 2018, in Kididiwe, joint FARDC-MONUSCO troops recaptured a former FARDC camp from ADF militiamen. During the operation, 7 United Nations peacekeepers were killed and 10 wounded, while 2 were still missing in action. FARDC reported that 14 soldiers had been killed, 29 had been wounded and 12 were still missing in action. An estimated 40 to 50 ADF combatants were killed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13070)

desc: 15 September. Between 2 and 4 am, police apprehended seven males, including teenagers, from the Kauka neighborhood of Kalamu. Five of the detainees were found dead the following day, at least one of the victim's genitals were cut off. The two others were forcibly disappeared. The attack is believed to be part of operations targeting Kulunas and political opposition against Kabila. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13648)

desc: On 24 August 2018, presumed ADF rebels attacked the FARDC base in Ngadi village (Beni, Nord-Kivu) causing panic in the locals, who fled for Beni. Seventeen soldiers, including a major, and one civilian were killed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC12960)

desc: On 24 August 2018, the ADF attacked an FARDC position in the Ngadi neighborhood of Beni (Beni, Nord-Kivu). This attack resulted in seventeen FARDC soldiers killed, six FARDC soldiers wounded and one missing. The ADF looted weapons and the money that was intended pay salaries for soldiers. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC45582)

desc: On 9 August 2018, the Mai-Mai Sheikh Assan (Malaika) attacked a Simba company convoy escorted by the FARDC near Mayi ya Moto village, 8 Km from Namoya (Kabambare, Maniema). Two FARDC soldiers and two civilians were killed while another civilian was wounded. The attackers kidnapped seven men and stole money and property. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13814)

desc: On 8 August 2018, at least 12 civilians were killed in Kiniana, Kalungu locality, close to Kitchanga, North Kivu, during clashes between the NDC / Renove de Guidon militias and the Nyatura. According to the local police, who delivers the information, some victims have already been buried. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC12831)

desc: On 7 August 2018, the ADF killed 13 people from a group of hostages who had been abducted on 3 Aug 2018 in Tubameme village, Beni territory. The bodies of the victims were found near Tubameme village. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13809)

desc: On 2 August 2018, thirteen farmers were kidnapped by ADF rebels from their field in Mayangose village just outside Beni city (Beni, Nord-Kivu). One farmer alerted the FARDC who tried unsuccessfully to pursue the attackers. The following Tuesday on 7 August fourteen lifeless decaying bodies were found and presumed to be those abducted who were earlier abducted. The assailants reportedly took with them 3 children after killing the adults. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC13130)

desc: On the night of May 20-21, an armed group attacked the village of Matopolo, in the grouping Kalamba Dilondo, in Mweka, and killed 8 people all belonging to the Mpiang group. 13 others were injured during the attack (MSF mentions 12 dead according to official sources). The attackers are presumably from the Kete community. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC12349)

actor1: ADF: Allied Democratic Forces
title: On 20 May 2018, presumed ADF rebels attacked Mbau northeast of Beni. 9 civilians were killed during the attack and 2 injured. Radio Okapi and AFP ment
desc: On 20 May 2018, presumed ADF rebels attacked Mbau northeast of Beni. 9 civilians were killed during the attack and 2 injured. Radio Okapi and AFP mention that casualties were 10, later confirmed by the army and civil society. The attack started at 18h30, with rebels burning a vehicle, pillaging stores and businesses, and killing civilians who inhabited the location. The military eventually intervened and the clash lasted for 3h, with the FARDC managing in the end to repel them (coded in a separate battle event). The MONUSCO aided during the pursuit. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC12345)

desc: On 12 May 2018, in unspecified location [coded to Bangadi], unidentified men armed with knives killed 1 boy and 1 young man from the same family. Neighbors reported that the assailants, dressed in robes, killed the boys when they refused to give the cassava, groundnuts, rice, and corn they were transporting. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC14545)

desc: On 11 April 2018, a group of armed men tried tried to rob civilians in Lubumbashi. After trying to rob money and not managing, they decided to return, but their passage was blocked by a cadet. The armed bandits turned violent and shot him 4 times, apart from injuring his sister. The cadet died and his sister died as well due to the wounds. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC12172)
