desc: A UN investigation carried out in March 2018 revealed that over 16 December - 14 March, 263 people were killed in violence between the Lendu and Hema ethnic groups, 29 injured and 120 localities and villages burnt down. Most of the victims hail from the Hema group. The violence took place in Djugu territory (Bahema-Nord, Walendu Tatsi, Walendu Djatsi, Walendu Pitsi, Bahema Banyuagi, Ndo Okebo and Mambisa). 163 coded in individual events in dataset; additional 100 coded in general events for December, January, February and March. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC12203)

title: On 25 February 2018, protests called by the CLC to denounce the prolonged stay of President Kabila took place in Kinshasa ( Kinshasa, Kinshasa) and a
desc: On 25 February 2018, protests called by the CLC to denounce the prolonged stay of President Kabila took place in Kinshasa ( Kinshasa, Kinshasa) and a number of cities across DRC despite being banned by the government. Police fired live bullets and tear gas in Kinshasa leaving two protesters seriously injured and two others killed. The DRC government blocked all access to internet and SMS during the protests. MONUSCO reported 100 arrests during the protests. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC12006)

title: 25 February 2018. In Mbandaka, CLC demonstrators attacked the police during the CLC demonstrations. 4 security officers were injured, as well as 4 dem
desc: 25 February 2018. In Mbandaka, CLC demonstrators attacked the police during the CLC demonstrations. 4 security officers were injured, as well as 4 demonstrators. One of the demonstrators was killed by a police officer while he was returning home. The police officer then fled the scene. In response to the killing, the youth set the police training centre in Mbandaka and the houses of a major and policeman on fire. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC12001)

desc: On 16 February 2018, 4 people were killed 15km away from Ango by unidentified armed men (suspected to be either the LRA or Mbororos breeders). Since the LRA had attacked the cities of Mobili and Wepa the day before, and they are 15 and 25 km from the centre of Ango, it is likely that it was them. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC11976)

desc: The Congolese and Rwandan forces clashed on February 13 when the FARDC were engaged in combat in Isoke, National Park of Virunga, in Rutshuru. The FARDC initially thought it was the ex-M23, but it was in fact the Rwandan forces. There were deaths on both sides, with 6 on the FARDC side (death toll later increased to 10; and another 4 injured) and others in the Rwandan side (although no numbers were confirmed). desc: On 6 February 2018, a suspected LRA member ambushed a motorcycle taxi driver. After that, he shot and killed another motorcycle driver who drove by and refused to stop. The assailant was then joined by two others, who proceeded to rob both drivers. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC11910)

actor1: Hema Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Lendu Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
title: On 3 February 2018, inter-ethnic fighting broke out in Blukwa, around 100km from Bunia, between the Hema and Lendu militias. The attack lasted two day
desc: On 3 February 2018, inter-ethnic fighting broke out in Blukwa, around 100km from Bunia, between the Hema and Lendu militias. The attack lasted two days and at least 23 members of the Hema community were killed by Lendu (although reports differ, ranging from 23-32), 12 people were severely injured and several houses were burned during the attack on the Hema village. Police and soldiers were dispatched to the spot to prevent the spread of violence, which followed days of tension between the two ethnic communities in the region. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC11902)

desc: On 21 January 2018, protests organised by the CLC in Kinshasa (Kinshasa , Kinshasa ) were choked by police, who used excessive force. Live bullets were shot near the St. Gabriel de Yolo Sud parish (commune of Kalamu), and one protester was injured. Protesters at Saint Jean-Baptiste parish were also dispersed with tear gas and live bullets; one protester was beaten up. MONUSCO reports that 6 protesters were killed, including a 16 year old girl, along with 57 people injured and more than 100 people arrested nationwide. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC11838)

desc: ADF attacked 2 locations near the Uganda border on Friday 19th - the villages of Parikingi and Abialos, resulting in the death of 4 rebels and 20 FARDC soldiers. 4 AK-47s were recovered, as well as a Motorola radio and a mortar. desc: Reports of violence against civilians in the form of assaults with machetes owing to intercommunal violence are causing people to flee Djugu and the region of Ituri. A UN investigation carried out in March 2018 revealed that over 16 December - 14 March, 263 people were killed in violence between the Lendu and Hema ethnic groups, 29 injured and 120 localities and villages burnt down. Most of the victims hail from the Hema group. The violence took place in Djugu territory (Bahema-Nord, Walendu Tatsi, Walendu Djatsi, Walendu Pitsi, Bahema Banyuagi, Ndo Okebo and Mambisa). 163 fatalities coded in individual events in dataset; additional 100 coded in general events for December, January, February and March. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC11740)

Air force and long-range artillery were used (operation Tuugo). This came after the establishment of a mechanism to share information on ADF with the DRC forces. desc: Four people were injured during clashes with the police on Wednesday 15th. Although it started as a peaceful protest, police clashed with protesters, firing live bullets. Accounts differ on the motivations for the protest. On the one side, it is said that protesters were students demanding the resume of their classes; their teachers had been in strike for a week due to their unpaid salaries. On the other end, the protesters are said to be part of a civilian movement in Lusambo to denounce the mismanagement of Governor Berthold Ulungu. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC11623)

desc: Clashes between Congolese troops and supporters of a renegade colonel (Abbas Kayonga) in Congo's eastern city of Bukavu killed seven people on Sunday before he surrendered and turned himself in to U.N. Two army soldiers and three of Kayonga's bodyguards were killed in the fighting and two civilians were killed by stray bullets, he said. desc: On 15 September 2017, in Kamanyola, FARDC and PNC elements opened fire on Burundian asylum seekers, who were allegedly rioting (reported by Radio Okapi), killing 38 people. Five soldiers and a police officer have been presented on trial on 30 July 2019 on charges of murder and attempted murder for the incident. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC15117)

desc: Cinq village in Kasai was attacked by Bana Mura militia organised by FARDC commanders. Luba and Lulua villagers were rounded up and shot or killed by machetes. Villagers who tried to escape were stopped at a military checkpoint on the road out of the village and killed or raped. Bana Mura invaded the hospital, killing some patients and medical personnel. The UN has corroborated 116 deaths in Cinq. Many of the villagers who escaped have been mutilated. The few Kamwina present in the village fled, without reported clashes with the Bana Mura. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC11058)

desc: Police and suspected BDK members clashed in Kimpese. title: A group of demobilized APCLS fighters who were quarted at the Kamina military base are suspected of having buried alive 8 other demobilized fighters f
desc: A group of demobilized APCLS fighters who were quarted at the Kamina military base are suspected of having buried alive 8 other demobilized fighters from the Ngiti ethnic group as they believed they were being favoured in the selection process for reintegration. At least 1 of those who were buried escaped and later testified against the group. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC10492)

actor1: Rioters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2001-2019)
title: Continued clashes in Kinshasa between large numbers of protesters and police over election delays resulted in 15 killed, while 49 civilians were said
desc: Continued clashes in Kinshasa between large numbers of protesters and police over election delays resulted in 15 killed, while 49 civilians were said to have been killed in total. As many as 3 more police were allegedly killed in the second day of violence. A police station and 12 other police positions were looted and set on fire by rioters according to police. About 126 injuries were also reported, across the protests in Kinshasa, Beni, Matadi and Goma. As many as 368 people were arrested by the PNC, FARDC and ANR (intelligence agency) across protests in Bandundu, Beni, Goma, Kindu, Kisangani, Kinshasa, Matadi and Tshikapa. These totals were according to the UN office for Human Rights. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC10488)

actor1: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2001-2019)

desc: After the UDPS headquarters was allegedly stormed and burned by police forces during the night, 3 charred bodies were found in the wreckage the next morning. At least 2 others opposition HQs were also reportedly burned during the night, including those of FONUS and the MLP. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC10489)

actor1: Rioters (Democratic Republic of Congo)
actor2: Police Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2001-2019)
title: 14 civilians and 3 police officers were reported killed in clashes which broke out during protests organized by the OCR against elections delays by Pr
desc: 14 civilians and 3 police officers were reported killed in clashes which broke out during protests organized by the OCR against elections delays by President Kabilas government. One of the police officers was allegedly burnt alive. The protests were reported to have drawn thousands of participants. The HQs of several of the ruling coalitions parties (including the RCD,PPRD, ATD, ARC and CNC) were also burnt down by opposition demonstrators, but no casualties were reported from these acts. 49 civilians were said to have been killed in the violence in Kinshasa in total. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC10481)

title: Residents of Kavumu in Kabare captured an alleged goat thief and took him to the police. After the police refused to allow the youths who captured the
desc: Residents of Kavumu in Kabare captured an alleged goat thief and took him to the police. After the police refused to allow the youths who captured the thief to lynch him, they fought with the police, secured the thief, and burned him alive and set the police station on fire. Military reinforcements dispersed the arsonists. During the events at least one person was hit by a bullet and killed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC10476)

desc: At least 1 person was killed in clashes between UDPS supporters and police forces in Kinshasa. The fight allegedly started when a police officer was passing the UDPS HQ and was attacked by activists, which led to the officer firing into the crowd while trying to escape. During the incident a bus was torched; police also used tear gas to try to disperse the crowds. There was also an unverified report that a police officer was killed. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC10347)

desc: Motorcycle taxi drivers staged a protest-strike in Kisangani against alleged arbitrary arrests and harassment against them by agents of the cities traffic police. They built a barricade on roads leading to the city centre and allegedly trashed a police station and some businesses. Some protesters engaging in a march were also dispersed by police using tear gas. At some point during the events, 3 protesters were allegedly killed during clashes with police along with several injured, including policemen. The police allegedly opened fire on the protesters. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC10328)

actor1: M23: March 23 Movement
actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2001-2019)
title: At least 11 people were killed and others wounded as clashes broke out between demobilized combatants and FARDC soldiers in Kamina. The FARDC soldiers
desc: At least 11 people were killed and others wounded as clashes broke out between demobilized combatants and FARDC soldiers in Kamina. The FARDC soldiers allegedly opened fire on thousands of demobilized M23 combatants who were protesting against the delays surrounding their reintegration by the military authorities, killing at least 10 and wounding 4. A FARDC officer was also killed with a machete by the former combatants, and 4 other soldiers wounded. The former combatants allegedly tried to invade and loot the Kamina city centre. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC10227)

desc: Around 500 motorcycle drivers protested on the main road of Niania, in the Isrio-Mambasa territory, over alleged demands of bribes by the Road Traffic Police (PCR). actor1: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2001-2019)
title: On 30 November 2015, ADF rebels dislodged FARDC and MONUSCO regained control of Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu) during clashes that started on the previous
desc: On 30 November 2015, ADF rebels dislodged FARDC and MONUSCO regained control of Eringeti (Beni, Nord-Kivu) during clashes that started on the previous day (coded separately). The rebels attack stretched from Makembi to Eringeti and simultaneously targeted a FARDC position, a MONUSCO base, a hospital in Eringeti, and the office of the PNC in Beni territory. During the 2 days of clashes, 1 UN peacekeeper, 7 soldiers, 10 civilians and 21 rebels were killed, while 1 peacekeeper and 4 soldiers were injured. 5 more people believe to have been killed during the fighting were found a few days later. During the violence at least 43 homes were also burned. At least 2,000 people have fled following the fighting. Fatalities coded as 44 (1 UN PKO, 7 soldiers, 10 civilians, 21 rebels and 5 more people), split into 2 events, coded as 22 in this event. (来源: ACLED, 事件ID: DRC9730)
